papers.title: ClawGUI: A Unified Framework for Training, Evaluating, and Deploying GUI Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract ClawGUI presents an open-source framework that addresses key challenges in GUI agent development through unified reinforcement learning, standardized evaluation, and cross-platform deployment capabilities.  AI-generated summary GUI agents drive applications through their visual interfaces instead of programmatic APIs, interacting with arbitrary software via taps, swipes, and keystrokes, reaching a long tail of applications that CLI-based agents cannot. Yet progress in this area is bottlenecked less by modeling capacity than by the absence of a coherent full-stack infrastructure: online RL training suffers from environment instability and closed pipelines, evaluation protocols drift silently across works, and trained agents rarely reach real users on real devices. We present ClawGUI, an open-source framework addressing these three gaps within a single harness. ClawGUI-RL provides the first open-source GUI agent RL infrastructure with validated support for both parallel virtual environments and real physical devices, integrating GiGPO with a Process Reward Model for dense step-level supervision. ClawGUI-Eval enforces a fully standardized evaluation pipeline across 6 benchmarks and 11+ models, achieving 95.8\% reproduction against official baselines. ClawGUI-Agent brings trained agents to Android, HarmonyOS, and iOS through 12+ chat platforms with hybrid CLI-GUI control and persistent personalized memory. Trained end to end within this pipeline, ClawGUI-2B achieves 17.1\% Success Rate on MobileWorld GUI-Only, outperforming the same-scale MAI-UI-2B baseline by 6.0\%.

ru.title: Единая инфраструктура для обучения и развертывания GUI-агентов в реальном мире
ru.desc: ClawGUI — это открытый фреймворк для разработки GUI-агентов, которые взаимодействуют с приложениями через визуальные интерфейсы вместо программных API. Фреймворк решает три ключевые проблемы: обеспечивает стабильную инфраструктуру для обучения с подкреплением (RL) на виртуальных и реальных устройствах, внедряет стандартизированный протокол оценки моделей и позволяет развертывать обученные агенты на мобильных платформах через различные мессенджеры. ClawGUI использует метод GiGPO с моделью вознаграждения для плотного супервизирования на уровне отдельных действий, обеспечивая лучшую обучаемость агентов.

en.title: Empowering GUI Agents with Unified Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: ClawGUI is an open-source framework designed to enhance the development of GUI agents using reinforcement learning. It addresses critical issues such as environment instability during online training, inconsistent evaluation protocols, and the challenge of deploying trained agents to real devices. The framework includes ClawGUI-RL for robust training across virtual and physical environments, ClawGUI-Eval for standardized evaluation across multiple benchmarks, and ClawGUI-Agent for deploying agents on various mobile platforms. This comprehensive approach allows for improved performance and accessibility of AI-driven GUI agents, achieving notable success rates in real-world applications.

zh.title: ClawGUI：统一强化学习的开源GUI代理框架
zh.desc: ClawGUI是一个开源框架，旨在解决图形用户界面（GUI）代理开发中的关键挑战。它通过统一的强化学习、标准化评估和跨平台部署能力，推动AI生成的GUI代理通过视觉界面与软件交互。该框架提供了一个完整的基础设施，解决了在线强化学习训练中的环境不稳定性和评估协议不一致的问题。ClawGUI的多个组件使得训练的代理能够在多个操作系统上运行，并在真实设备上实现高效的用户交互。

papers.title: KnowRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning with Minimal-Sufficient Knowledge Guidance
papers.abstract: Abstract KnowRL is a knowledge-guided reinforcement learning framework that improves reasoning in language models by optimizing compact, interaction-aware guidance subsets through constrained subset search and addressing pruning interaction paradoxes.  AI-generated summary RLVR improves reasoning in large language models, but its effectiveness is often limited by severe reward sparsity on hard problems. Recent hint-based RL methods mitigate sparsity by injecting partial solutions or abstract templates, yet they typically scale guidance by adding more tokens, which introduce redundancy, inconsistency, and extra training overhead. We propose KnowRL (Knowledge-Guided Reinforcement Learning), an RL training framework that treats hint design as a minimal-sufficient guidance problem. During RL training, KnowRL decomposes guidance into atomic knowledge points (KPs) and uses Constrained Subset Search (CSS) to construct compact, interaction-aware subsets for training. We further identify a pruning interaction paradox -- removing one KP may help while removing multiple such KPs can hurt -- and explicitly optimize for robust subset curation under this dependency structure. We train KnowRL-Nemotron-1.5B from OpenMath-Nemotron-1.5B. Across eight reasoning benchmarks at the 1.5B scale, KnowRL-Nemotron-1.5B consistently outperforms strong RL and hinting baselines. Without KP hints at inference, KnowRL-Nemotron-1.5B reaches 70.08 average accuracy, already surpassing Nemotron-1.5B by +9.63 points; with selected KPs, performance improves to 74.16, establishing a new state of the art at this scale. The model, curated training data, and code are publicly available at https://github.com/Hasuer/KnowRL.
affiliations: Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences
TJUNLP Lab, School of Computer Science and Technology, Tianjin University, China

ru.title: Минимальные, но достаточные подсказки для усиленного обучения языковых моделей
ru.desc: KnowRL — это фреймворк для обучения языковых моделей с подкреплением, который улучшает способность к рассуждению, оптимизируя компактные подсказки через поиск минимального достаточного подмножества. Авторы разлагают подсказки на атомарные единицы знания и используют constrained subset search для построения взаимодействий-ориентированных подмножеств, которые минимизируют избыточность и несогласованность. Работа выявляет парадокс обрезки: удаление одной единицы знания может помочь, но удаление нескольких вредит качеству, поэтому авторы явно оптимизируют структуру зависимостей. Модель KnowRL-Nemotron-1.5B достигает новых результатов на восьми бенчмарках математического рассуждения, превосходя базовые модели на 9.63 пункта без подсказок и на 4.53 пункта с выбранными единицами знания.

en.title: Optimizing Knowledge for Smarter Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: KnowRL is a novel framework that enhances reasoning in language models through knowledge-guided reinforcement learning. It optimizes the selection of compact, interaction-aware subsets of knowledge points (KPs) to provide effective guidance during training. By addressing the pruning interaction paradox, KnowRL ensures that the removal of certain KPs can improve performance while maintaining overall robustness. The results show that KnowRL-Nemotron-1.5B significantly outperforms existing methods on reasoning tasks, achieving a new state of the art in accuracy.

zh.title: 知识引导的强化学习提升推理能力
zh.desc: KnowRL是一种知识引导的强化学习框架，旨在通过优化紧凑的、关注交互的指导子集来改善语言模型的推理能力。该框架通过约束子集搜索（CSS）将指导分解为原子知识点（KPs），并构建适合训练的子集。KnowRL还解决了修剪交互悖论的问题，即移除一个KP可能有益，但移除多个KP可能会造成负面影响。在多个推理基准测试中，KnowRL-Nemotron-1.5B模型的表现超越了其他强化学习和提示基线，展示了其在大规模推理任务中的优越性。

papers.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
papers.abstract: Abstract On-policy distillation dynamics in large language models depend on compatible thinking patterns between teacher and student models, with successful distillation characterized by alignment on high-probability tokens and requiring teachers to provide novel capabilities beyond student training data.  AI-generated summary On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-strong reverse distillation, showing that same-family 1.5B and 7B teachers are distributionally indistinguishable from the student's perspective. Probing into the token-level mechanism, we show that successful OPD is characterized by progressive alignment on high-probability tokens at student-visited states, a small shared token set that concentrates most of the probability mass (97%-99%). We further propose two practical strategies to recover failing OPD: off-policy cold start and teacher-aligned prompt selection. Finally, we show that OPD's apparent free lunch of dense token-level reward comes at a cost, raising the question of whether OPD can scale to long-horizon distillation.

ru.title: Совместимость мышления — ключ к успешной дистилляции больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется динамика дистилляции языковых моделей в режиме on-policy, где учитель передаёт знания студенту. Авторы обнаруживают два ключевых условия успеха: совместимость паттернов мышления между моделями и наличие у учителя подлинно новых возможностей, которых студент не видел в данных обучения. На уровне токенов успешная дистилляция характеризуется прогрессивным согласованием на высокий вероятных токенах, которые сосредоточены в малом наборе и занимают 97-99% вероятностной массы. Предложены практические методы восстановления неудачной дистилляции и поднимается вопрос о масштабируемости подхода на длинные горизонты.

en.title: Aligning Minds: The Key to Successful On-Policy Distillation
en.desc: This paper explores the dynamics of on-policy distillation (OPD) in large language models, focusing on the relationship between teacher and student models. It identifies two key conditions for successful distillation: the compatibility of thinking patterns between the models and the necessity for the teacher to introduce new capabilities that the student has not encountered. The authors demonstrate that effective OPD is marked by alignment on high-probability tokens, which are crucial for the student's learning process. Additionally, they propose strategies to improve failing OPD and discuss the challenges of scaling this technique for longer distillation tasks.

zh.title: 思维模式兼容性是蒸馏成功的关键
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在大型语言模型中，基于策略的蒸馏动态（OPD）的成功与否取决于教师模型和学生模型之间的思维模式兼容性。成功的蒸馏需要教师提供超出学生训练数据的新能力，即使在思维模式一致的情况下，教师也必须提供新的信息。研究表明，成功的OPD特征在于学生访问状态下高概率标记的逐步对齐，这些标记集中在一个小的共享标记集上。最后，提出了两种恢复失败OPD的实用策略，并讨论了OPD在长时间蒸馏中的可扩展性问题。

papers.title: Turing Test on Screen: A Benchmark for Mobile GUI Agent Humanization
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers propose humanization capabilities for autonomous GUI agents to avoid detection by digital platforms, introducing a benchmark and methods to balance imitability with task performance.  AI-generated summary The rise of autonomous GUI agents has triggered adversarial countermeasures from digital platforms, yet existing research prioritizes utility and robustness over the critical dimension of anti-detection. We argue that for agents to survive in human-centric ecosystems, they must evolve Humanization capabilities. We introduce the ``Turing Test on Screen,'' formally modeling the interaction as a MinMax optimization problem between a detector and an agent aiming to minimize behavioral divergence. We then collect a new high-fidelity dataset of mobile touch dynamics, and conduct our analysis that vanilla LMM-based agents are easily detectable due to unnatural kinematics. Consequently, we establish the Agent Humanization Benchmark (AHB) and detection metrics to quantify the trade-off between imitability and utility. Finally, we propose methods ranging from heuristic noise to data-driven behavioral matching, demonstrating that agents can achieve high imitability theoretically and empirically without sacrificing performance. This work shifts the paradigm from whether an agent can perform a task to how it performs it within a human-centric ecosystem, laying the groundwork for seamless coexistence in adversarial digital environments.

ru.title: Как агент может скрыться в мире людей: баланс между незаметностью и эффективностью
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали методы для обучения автономных GUI агентов, работающих с экранами, чтобы они имитировали человеческое поведение и избегали обнаружения системами защиты платформ. Авторы формализуют эту задачу как минимаксную оптимизацию между детектором и агентом, стремящимся минимизировать различия в поведении. Они собрали набор данных о динамике касаний на мобильных устройствах и выявили, что обычные LLM-агенты легко обнаруживаются из-за неестественной кинематики движений. Предложены методы от эвристического шумообогащения до обучения с подкреплением на реальных данных, позволяющие агентам достичь высокую имитируемость без снижения производительности.

en.title: Humanizing AI Agents for Undetectable Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces the concept of humanization for autonomous GUI agents to help them avoid detection by digital platforms. The authors present a new benchmark called the Agent Humanization Benchmark (AHB) to measure the balance between how well agents imitate human behavior and their task performance. They model the interaction between agents and detectors as a MinMax optimization problem, highlighting the need for agents to minimize behavioral divergence. The study also provides methods to enhance agents' human-like behavior, demonstrating that it is possible to improve their imitability without compromising their effectiveness in tasks.

zh.title: 提升自主代理的人性化能力，避免数字检测
zh.desc: 本研究提出了自主图形用户界面（GUI）代理的人性化能力，以避免被数字平台检测。我们引入了“屏幕上的图灵测试”，将代理与检测器之间的互动建模为一个最小最大优化问题。通过收集高保真度的移动触控动态数据集，我们发现传统的基于线性模型的代理因运动不自然而容易被检测。最后，我们建立了代理人性化基准（AHB）和检测指标，提出了多种方法来平衡模仿性与任务性能。

papers.title: SPPO: Sequence-Level PPO for Long-Horizon Reasoning Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract Sequence-Level PPO addresses instability in long-chain-of-thought reasoning by reformulating the process as a contextual bandit problem with decoupled value functions for improved efficiency.  AI-generated summary Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) is central to aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) in reasoning tasks with verifiable rewards. However, standard token-level PPO struggles in this setting due to the instability of temporal credit assignment over long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) horizons and the prohibitive memory cost of the value model. While critic-free alternatives like GRPO mitigate these issues, they incur significant computational overhead by requiring multiple samples for baseline estimation, severely limiting training throughput. In this paper, we introduce Sequence-Level PPO (SPPO), a scalable algorithm that harmonizes the sample efficiency of PPO with the stability of outcome-based updates. SPPO reformulates the reasoning process as a Sequence-Level Contextual Bandit problem, employing a decoupled scalar value function to derive low-variance advantage signals without multi-sampling. Extensive experiments on mathematical benchmarks demonstrate that SPPO significantly surpasses standard PPO and matches the performance of computation-heavy group-based methods, offering a resource-efficient framework for aligning reasoning LLMs.
affiliations: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications
Shanghai University of Finance and Economics
Southern University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Стабильное обучение с подкреплением для длинных цепочек рассуждений
ru.desc: В статье предлагается Sequence-Level PPO (SPPO) — алгоритм обучения с подкреплением для стабилизации длинных цепочек рассуждений в больших языковых моделях. Авторы переформулируют задачу как контекстную многорукую машину (contextual bandit), что позволяет избежать проблем с кредитным назначением на длинных горизонтах. Ключевым нововведением является использование развязанной скалярной функции ценности, которая генерирует сигналы преимущества с низкой дисперсией без необходимости множественной выборки. Экспериментальные результаты на математических эталонах показывают, что SPPO превосходит стандартный PPO и конкурирует с ресурсоёмкими групповыми методами.

en.title: Enhancing Reasoning Efficiency with Sequence-Level PPO
en.desc: This paper presents Sequence-Level PPO (SPPO), a new algorithm designed to improve the stability and efficiency of reasoning tasks in Large Language Models (LLMs). By treating the reasoning process as a contextual bandit problem, SPPO uses decoupled value functions to provide low-variance advantage signals without the need for multiple samples. This approach addresses the challenges of temporal credit assignment and high memory costs associated with traditional token-level Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). Experimental results show that SPPO outperforms standard PPO and competes effectively with more resource-intensive methods, making it a promising solution for aligning LLMs in complex reasoning scenarios.

zh.title: 序列级PPO：高效稳定的推理对齐方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的算法，称为序列级PPO（SPPO），旨在解决长链推理中的不稳定性问题。SPPO将推理过程重新构建为上下文赌博问题，并使用解耦的价值函数来提高效率。与传统的PPO相比，SPPO在样本效率和结果更新的稳定性方面表现更佳，避免了多次采样带来的计算开销。实验结果表明，SPPO在数学基准测试中显著超越了标准PPO，并与计算密集型的基于组的方法相匹配，提供了一种资源高效的框架。

papers.title: Toward Autonomous Long-Horizon Engineering for ML Research
papers.abstract: Abstract AiScientist enables autonomous long-horizon ML research engineering by combining hierarchical orchestration with durable state management, achieving superior performance on benchmark tasks through structured coordination and persistent project artifacts.  AI-generated summary Autonomous AI research has advanced rapidly, but long-horizon ML research engineering remains difficult: agents must sustain coherent progress across task comprehension, environment setup, implementation, experimentation, and debugging over hours or days. We introduce AiScientist, a system for autonomous long-horizon engineering for ML research built on a simple principle: strong long-horizon performance requires both structured orchestration and durable state continuity. To this end, AiScientist combines hierarchical orchestration with a permission-scoped File-as-Bus workspace: a top-level Orchestrator maintains stage-level control through concise summaries and a workspace map, while specialized agents repeatedly re-ground on durable artifacts such as analyses, plans, code, and experimental evidence rather than relying primarily on conversational handoffs, yielding thin control over thick state. Across two complementary benchmarks, AiScientist improves PaperBench score by 10.54 points on average over the best matched baseline and achieves 81.82 Any Medal% on MLE-Bench Lite. Ablation studies further show that File-as-Bus protocol is a key driver of performance, reducing PaperBench by 6.41 points and MLE-Bench Lite by 31.82 points when removed. These results suggest that long-horizon ML research engineering is a systems problem of coordinating specialized work over durable project state, rather than a purely local reasoning problem.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Долгосрочные ML-исследования как проблема координации систем, а не рассуждения
ru.desc: AiScientist — это система для автономного проведения долгосрочных исследований в области машинного обучения, которая комбинирует иерархическую оркестрацию агентов с устойчивым управлением состоянием проекта. Система использует принцип «File-as-Bus», где специализированные агенты многократно обращаются к долговечным артефактам (анализы, планы, код, результаты экспериментов) вместо передачи информации через диалоги. Верхний уровень Orchestrator поддерживает контроль над этапами через краткие резюме и карту рабочего пространства, что позволяет достичь тонкого управления над сложным состоянием проекта. На бенчмарках PaperBench и MLE-Bench система показала значительное улучшение производительности, что доказывает, что долгосрочные ML-исследования — это скорее проблема систем координации, чем локального рассуждения.

en.title: Empowering Autonomous ML Research with Structured Coordination
en.desc: AiScientist is a system designed to enhance autonomous long-horizon machine learning (ML) research by integrating hierarchical orchestration with durable state management. This approach allows agents to maintain consistent progress across various stages of research, including task understanding, environment setup, and experimentation. By utilizing a File-as-Bus workspace, AiScientist enables specialized agents to access and build upon persistent project artifacts, leading to improved coordination and efficiency. The system has demonstrated significant performance improvements on benchmark tasks, indicating that effective long-horizon ML research requires a focus on structured collaboration and sustained project continuity.

zh.title: 自主长时间机器学习研究的未来
zh.desc: AiScientist 是一个用于自主长时间机器学习研究工程的系统，它结合了分层协调和持久状态管理。该系统通过结构化的协调和持久的项目文档，显著提高了基准任务的性能。AiScientist 的核心理念是，强大的长时间表现需要有条理的协调和持续的状态连续性。实验结果表明，File-as-Bus 协议是提升性能的关键因素，表明长时间机器学习研究工程是一个协调专业工作的系统问题，而不仅仅是局部推理问题。

papers.title: BERT-as-a-Judge: A Robust Alternative to Lexical Methods for Efficient Reference-Based LLM Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language model evaluation faces challenges with rigid lexical methods that confuse problem-solving ability with formatting compliance, prompting the introduction of BERT-as-a-Judge for more robust, scalable assessment of generative outputs.  AI-generated summary Accurate evaluation is central to the large language model (LLM) ecosystem, guiding model selection and downstream adoption across diverse use cases. In practice, however, evaluating generative outputs typically relies on rigid lexical methods to extract and assess answers, which can conflate a model's true problem-solving ability with its compliance with predefined formatting guidelines. While recent LLM-as-a-Judge approaches mitigate this issue by assessing semantic correctness rather than strict structural conformity, they also introduce substantial computational overhead, making evaluation costly. In this work, we first systematically investigate the limitations of lexical evaluation through a large-scale empirical study spanning 36 models and 15 downstream tasks, demonstrating that such methods correlate poorly with human judgments. To address this limitation, we introduce BERT-as-a-Judge, an encoder-driven approach for assessing answer correctness in reference-based generative settings, robust to variations in output phrasing, and requiring only lightweight training on synthetically annotated question-candidate-reference triplets. We show that it consistently outperforms the lexical baseline while matching the performance of much larger LLM judges, providing a compelling tradeoff between the two and enabling reliable, scalable evaluation. Finally, through extensive experimentation, we provide detailed insights into BERT-as-a-Judge's performance to offer practical guidance for practitioners, and release all project artifacts to foster downstream adoption.

ru.title: Лёгкий судья: эффективная оценка LLM без лишних затрат
ru.desc: В работе авторы показывают, что лексические методы оценки больших языковых моделей дают неправильные результаты, так как путают реальное понимание задачи с соответствием формату ответа. Они предлагают BERT-as-a-Judge — энкодер-подход на основе BERT для оценки корректности ответов, который устойчив к различным формулировкам и требует минимальных вычислительных ресурсов. Метод обучается на синтетических данных и показывает результаты, сравнимые с оценками больших моделей, но с намного меньшей вычислительной стоимостью. Это решение позволяет надёжно и масштабируемо оценивать выходы генеративных моделей в различных прикладных задачах.

en.title: BERT-as-a-Judge: A Smarter Way to Evaluate Language Models
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges in evaluating large language models (LLMs) by highlighting the limitations of traditional lexical evaluation methods, which often confuse a model's problem-solving skills with its adherence to formatting rules. The authors propose a new approach called BERT-as-a-Judge, which focuses on assessing the semantic correctness of generative outputs rather than their structural compliance. This method is designed to be more efficient and scalable, requiring less computational power while still providing reliable evaluations. Through extensive testing, BERT-as-a-Judge demonstrates superior performance compared to lexical methods and matches the effectiveness of larger LLM judges, making it a valuable tool for practitioners in the field.

zh.title: BERT作为评判者：提升语言模型评估的准确性
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了大型语言模型（LLM）评估中的挑战，尤其是传统的词汇评估方法如何混淆模型的实际问题解决能力与格式合规性。为了解决这个问题，作者提出了BERT-as-a-Judge，这是一种基于编码器的方法，用于在生成性设置中评估答案的正确性。该方法对输出措辞的变化具有鲁棒性，并且只需在合成标注的问答对上进行轻量级训练。实验结果表明，BERT-as-a-Judge在评估准确性上优于传统的词汇基线，同时与更大规模的LLM评估者的表现相当，提供了可靠且可扩展的评估方案。

papers.title: Lyra 2.0: Explorable Generative 3D Worlds
papers.abstract: Abstract Lyra 2.0 enables large-scale 3D scene creation through persistent video generation that addresses spatial forgetting and temporal drifting issues in long-horizon video models.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in video generation enable a new paradigm for 3D scene creation: generating camera-controlled videos that simulate scene walkthroughs, then lifting them to 3D via feed-forward reconstruction techniques. This generative reconstruction approach combines the visual fidelity and creative capacity of video models with 3D outputs ready for real-time rendering and simulation. Scaling to large, complex environments requires 3D-consistent video generation over long camera trajectories with large viewpoint changes and location revisits, a setting where current video models degrade quickly. Existing methods for long-horizon generation are fundamentally limited by two forms of degradation: spatial forgetting and temporal drifting. As exploration proceeds, previously observed regions fall outside the model's temporal context, forcing the model to hallucinate structures when revisited. Meanwhile, autoregressive generation accumulates small synthesis errors over time, gradually distorting scene appearance and geometry. We present Lyra 2.0, a framework for generating persistent, explorable 3D worlds at scale. To address spatial forgetting, we maintain per-frame 3D geometry and use it solely for information routing -- retrieving relevant past frames and establishing dense correspondences with the target viewpoints -- while relying on the generative prior for appearance synthesis. To address temporal drifting, we train with self-augmented histories that expose the model to its own degraded outputs, teaching it to correct drift rather than propagate it. Together, these enable substantially longer and 3D-consistent video trajectories, which we leverage to fine-tune feed-forward reconstruction models that reliably recover high-quality 3D scenes.

ru.title: Персистентная генерация видео для создания масштабных 3D миров
ru.desc: Lyra 2.0 — это фреймворк для генерации долгосрочных видео с сохранением пространственной согласованности, который решает проблемы забывания пройденных областей и накопления ошибок при длительной генерации. Система использует геометрию каждого кадра для маршрутизации информации, извлекая релевантные прошлые кадры и устанавливая соответствия между точками зрения, при этом полагаясь на генеративный приоритет видеомодели для синтеза текстур. Для борьбы с дрейфом во времени применяется обучение с самоувеличенными историями, которые учат модель исправлять накопленные ошибки вместо их распространения. Полученные видеопоследовательности с высокой 3D-согласованностью затем используются для тонкой настройки feed-forward моделей реконструкции, позволяя создавать высококачественные 3D сцены в масштабе.

en.title: Creating Persistent 3D Worlds with Lyra 2.0
en.desc: Lyra 2.0 is a framework designed to enhance the generation of large-scale 3D scenes by addressing common issues in long-horizon video models, specifically spatial forgetting and temporal drifting. It generates camera-controlled videos that simulate walkthroughs of 3D environments and then reconstructs these videos into 3D formats using advanced techniques. By maintaining 3D geometry for each frame and using it to retrieve relevant past frames, Lyra 2.0 ensures that previously observed areas are accurately represented when revisited. Additionally, it employs self-augmented training to help the model learn to correct errors over time, resulting in longer and more consistent video trajectories that produce high-quality 3D reconstructions.

zh.title: Lyra 2.0：持久视频生成的3D场景创作新纪元
zh.desc: Lyra 2.0 是一种新框架，能够通过持久的视频生成技术来创建大规模的3D场景。它解决了在长时间视频模型中常见的空间遗忘和时间漂移问题。该方法结合了视频模型的视觉真实感和创意能力，生成可实时渲染的3D输出。通过维护每帧的3D几何信息和自我增强的历史训练，Lyra 2.0 能够生成更长且一致的3D视频轨迹，从而可靠地恢复高质量的3D场景。

papers.title: Nemotron 3 Super: Open, Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Model for Agentic Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Nemotron 3 Super is a 120 billion parameter hybrid Mamba-Attention Mixture-of-Experts model pre-trained in NVFP4 with LatentMoE architecture and MTP layers for accelerated inference, achieving superior throughput compared to existing models.  AI-generated summary We describe the pre-training, post-training, and quantization of Nemotron 3 Super, a 120 billion (active 12 billion) parameter hybrid Mamba-Attention Mixture-of-Experts model. Nemotron 3 Super is the first model in the Nemotron 3 family to 1) be pre-trained in NVFP4, 2) leverage LatentMoE, a new Mixture-of-Experts architecture that optimizes for both accuracy per FLOP and accuracy per parameter, and 3) include MTP layers for inference acceleration through native speculative decoding. We pre-trained Nemotron 3 Super on 25 trillion tokens followed by post-training using supervised fine tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). The final model supports up to 1M context length and achieves comparable accuracy on common benchmarks, while also achieving up to 2.2x and 7.5x higher inference throughput compared to GPT-OSS-120B and Qwen3.5-122B, respectively. Nemotron 3 Super datasets, along with the base, post-trained, and quantized checkpoints, are open-sourced on HuggingFace.

ru.title: Максимальная скорость инференса через гибридную архитектуру и экспертные смеси
ru.desc: Nemotron 3 Super — это гибридная модель с 120 миллиардами параметров, комбинирующая архитектуру Mamba-Attention с механизмом Mixture-of-Experts для эффективной обработки. Модель предварительно обучена на 25 триллионах токенов в формате NVFP4 и использует инновационную архитектуру LatentMoE, которая оптимизирует баланс между точностью на единицу вычислений и точностью на параметр. Дополнительно модель включает MTP-слои, которые ускоряют инференс через встроенное спекулятивное декодирование и поддерживают контекст длиной до 1 миллиона токенов. Nemotron 3 Super демонстрирует превосходную пропускную способность — в 2.2 и 7.5 раза выше, чем у конкурентных моделей, при сохранении сравнимой точности на стандартных бенчмарках.

en.title: Revolutionizing Inference with Nemotron 3 Super!
en.desc: Nemotron 3 Super is a cutting-edge machine learning model with 120 billion parameters, designed using a hybrid Mamba-Attention Mixture-of-Experts architecture. It utilizes LatentMoE to enhance both accuracy and efficiency, allowing it to perform better with fewer active parameters. The model was pre-trained on a massive dataset of 25 trillion tokens and further refined through supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning. With its advanced MTP layers, Nemotron 3 Super achieves significantly higher inference throughput compared to existing models, making it a powerful tool for various applications.

zh.title: Nemotron 3 Super：高效推理的新标杆
zh.desc: Nemotron 3 Super 是一个拥有 1200 亿参数的混合 Mamba-Attention Mixture-of-Experts 模型，采用 NVFP4 进行预训练。该模型引入了 LatentMoE 架构，优化了每个 FLOP 和每个参数的准确性，并通过 MTP 层加速推理过程。它在 25 万亿个标记上进行预训练，并通过监督微调和强化学习进行后训练。最终模型支持高达 100 万的上下文长度，并在常见基准测试中表现出色，推理吞吐量比现有模型高出 2.2 倍到 7.5 倍。

papers.title: Towards Long-horizon Agentic Multimodal Search
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel long-horizon multimodal deep search framework called LMM-Searcher is introduced, featuring a file-based visual representation mechanism and progressive visual loading to handle heterogeneous information and high token costs effectively.  AI-generated summary Multimodal deep search agents have shown great potential in solving complex tasks by iteratively collecting textual and visual evidence. However, managing the heterogeneous information and high token costs associated with multimodal inputs over long horizons remains a critical challenge, as existing methods often suffer from context explosion or the loss of crucial visual signals. To address this, we propose a novel Long-horizon MultiModal deep search framework, named LMM-Searcher, centered on a file-based visual representation mechanism. By offloading visual assets to an external file system and mapping them to lightweight textual identifiers (UIDs), our approach mitigates context overhead while preserving multimodal information for future access. We equip the agent with a tailored fetch-image tool, enabling a progressive, on-demand visual loading strategy for active perception. Furthermore, we introduce a data synthesis pipeline designed to generate queries requiring complex cross-modal multi-hop reasoning. Using this pipeline, we distill 12K high-quality trajectories to fine-tune Qwen3-VL-Thinking-30A3B into a specialized multimodal deep search agent. Extensive experiments across four benchmarks demonstrate that our method successfully scales to 100-turn search horizons, achieving state-of-the-art performance among open-source models on challenging long-horizon benchmarks like MM-BrowseComp and MMSearch-Plus, while also exhibiting strong generalizability across different base models. Our code will be released in https://github.com/RUCAIBox/LMM-Searcher.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Эффективный поиск через файловое хранилище визуальных данных в мультимодальных агентах
ru.desc: В работе представлена система LMM-Searcher для решения сложных задач с использованием мультимодальных поисковых агентов, которые могут анализировать текст и изображения на протяжении многих итераций. Основная инновация заключается в механизме представления изображений на основе файловой системы, где визуальные данные хранятся отдельно и доступны через легкие текстовые идентификаторы, что снижает расходы на контекст. Система оснащена инструментом прогрессивной загрузки изображений для активного восприятия и использует синтетический конвейер данных для генерации запросов, требующих кросс-модальных многошаговых рассуждений. Метод достигает передовых результатов на эталонных наборах данных с горизонтом поиска до 100 шагов и показывает хорошую обобщаемость на различные базовые модели.

en.title: Revolutionizing Long-Horizon Multimodal Search with LMM-Searcher
en.desc: The paper presents LMM-Searcher, a new framework for long-horizon multimodal deep search that effectively manages complex tasks involving both text and images. It introduces a file-based visual representation system that reduces the burden of high token costs by linking visual assets to lightweight textual identifiers. This approach allows for progressive visual loading, enabling the agent to access visual information on demand without overwhelming the context. The framework is fine-tuned using a data synthesis pipeline, resulting in a multimodal search agent that excels in long-horizon tasks and demonstrates superior performance on various benchmarks.

zh.title: 长时域多模态深度搜索的新突破
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的长时域多模态深度搜索框架LMM-Searcher，旨在有效处理异构信息和高令牌成本。该框架采用基于文件的视觉表示机制，通过将视觉资产外部化并映射到轻量级文本标识符，减少了上下文开销。我们为代理配备了定制的图像获取工具，支持渐进式按需视觉加载策略，以增强主动感知能力。此外，本文还引入了一种数据合成管道，用于生成需要复杂跨模态多跳推理的查询。

papers.title: Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy in LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language model agents require robust instruction conflict resolution mechanisms that can handle arbitrary privilege levels across diverse real-world scenarios, revealing current models' limitations in managing complex hierarchical instructions.  AI-generated summary Large language model agents receive instructions from many sources-system messages, user prompts, tool outputs, and more-each carrying different levels of trust and authority. When these instructions conflict, models must reliably follow the highest-privilege instruction to remain safe and effective. The dominant paradigm, instruction hierarchy (IH), assumes a fixed, small set of privilege levels (typically fewer than five) defined by rigid role labels (e.g., system > user). This is inadequate for real-world agentic settings, where conflicts can arise across far more sources and contexts. In this work, we propose Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy (ManyIH), a paradigm for resolving instruction conflicts among instructions with arbitrarily many privilege levels. We introduce ManyIH-Bench, the first benchmark for ManyIH. ManyIH-Bench requires models to navigate up to 12 levels of conflicting instructions with varying privileges, comprising 853 agentic tasks (427 coding and 426 instruction-following). ManyIH-Bench composes constraints developed by LLMs and verified by humans to create realistic and difficult test cases spanning 46 real-world agents. Our experiments show that even the current frontier models perform poorly (~40% accuracy) when instruction conflict scales. This work underscores the urgent need for methods that explicitly target fine-grained, scalable instruction conflict resolution in agentic settings.

ru.title: Разрешение конфликтов многоуровневых инструкций в агентах LLM
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема разрешения конфликтов инструкций в агентах на основе больших языковых моделей. Авторы показывают, что традиционный подход иерархии инструкций с фиксированным набором уровней привилегий неадекватен для реальных сценариев, где может быть гораздо больше источников инструкций с разными приоритетами. Они предлагают парадигму Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy (ManyIH) для разрешения конфликтов между инструкциями с произвольным числом уровней привилегий и создают бенчмарк ManyIH-Bench с 853 задачами агентов. Эксперименты показывают, что даже передовые модели достигают лишь ~40% точности при масштабировании конфликтов инструкций, указывая на необходимость разработки новых методов для решения этой проблемы.

en.title: Empowering AI with Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy for Better Conflict Resolution
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by large language model agents when they encounter conflicting instructions from various sources with different levels of authority. Current models rely on a limited instruction hierarchy that cannot effectively manage the complexity of real-world scenarios where conflicts can arise from numerous sources. The authors propose a new framework called Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy (ManyIH) that allows for the resolution of conflicts among instructions with an arbitrary number of privilege levels. They also introduce ManyIH-Bench, a benchmark designed to test models on navigating up to 12 levels of conflicting instructions, highlighting the need for improved methods in instruction conflict resolution.

zh.title: 多层指令层次：解决复杂指令冲突的新方法
zh.desc: 大型语言模型代理需要强大的指令冲突解决机制，以处理多种现实场景中的不同权限级别。这些模型在管理复杂的层次指令时存在局限性，尤其是在指令冲突时，必须可靠地遵循最高权限的指令。本文提出了多层指令层次（ManyIH）这一新范式，旨在解决具有任意多个权限级别的指令冲突。我们还引入了ManyIH-Bench，这是第一个针对ManyIH的基准测试，要求模型处理多达12个权限级别的冲突指令，显示出当前模型在指令冲突扩展时的表现不佳。

papers.title: Habitat-GS: A High-Fidelity Navigation Simulator with Dynamic Gaussian Splatting
papers.abstract: Abstract Habitat-GS extends Habitat-Sim by integrating 3D Gaussian Splatting for photorealistic rendering and gaussian avatars for dynamic human modeling, enabling improved agent generalization and human-aware navigation in embodied AI.  AI-generated summary Training embodied AI agents depends critically on the visual fidelity of simulation environments and the ability to model dynamic humans. Current simulators rely on mesh-based rasterization with limited visual realism, and their support for dynamic human avatars, where available, is constrained to mesh representations, hindering agent generalization to human-populated real-world scenarios. We present Habitat-GS, a navigation-centric embodied AI simulator extended from Habitat-Sim that integrates 3D Gaussian Splatting scene rendering and drivable gaussian avatars while maintaining full compatibility with the Habitat ecosystem. Our system implements a 3DGS renderer for real-time photorealistic rendering and supports scalable 3DGS asset import from diverse sources. For dynamic human modeling, we introduce a gaussian avatar module that enables each avatar to simultaneously serve as a photorealistic visual entity and an effective navigation obstacle, allowing agents to learn human-aware behaviors in realistic settings. Experiments on point-goal navigation demonstrate that agents trained on 3DGS scenes achieve stronger cross-domain generalization, with mixed-domain training being the most effective strategy. Evaluations on avatar-aware navigation further confirm that gaussian avatars enable effective human-aware navigation. Finally, performance benchmarks validate the system's scalability across varying scene complexity and avatar counts.

ru.title: Фотореалистичная симуляция навигации с динамичными людьми
ru.desc: Habitat-GS расширяет симулятор Habitat-Sim, интегрируя метод 3D Gaussian Splatting для фотореалистичного рендеринга сцен и гауссовых аватаров для моделирования динамичных людей. Система позволяет агентам воплощённого AI обучаться навигации в визуально достоверных окружениях с реалистичными человеческими фигурами. Эксперименты показывают, что агенты, обученные на гауссовых сценах, лучше обобщаются на новые домены и демонстрируют эффективное избегание препятствий в виде людей. Решение сохраняет полную совместимость с экосистемой Habitat и масштабируется при увеличении сложности сцен и количества аватаров.

en.title: Enhancing AI Navigation with Photorealistic Environments and Dynamic Humans
en.desc: Habitat-GS is an advanced simulator for embodied AI that enhances visual realism and dynamic human modeling. It uses 3D Gaussian Splatting for photorealistic scene rendering, allowing agents to navigate in environments that closely resemble the real world. The introduction of gaussian avatars enables these agents to interact with realistic human representations, improving their ability to generalize across different scenarios. Experiments show that agents trained in this environment perform better in navigation tasks, especially when dealing with human-like obstacles.

zh.title: Habitat-GS：提升智能体导航的真实感与泛化能力
zh.desc: Habitat-GS 是一个增强版的模拟器，结合了 3D 高斯点云渲染技术和动态人类建模。它通过提供更真实的视觉效果，帮助智能体在复杂环境中更好地学习和导航。该系统支持实时渲染，并允许从多种来源导入 3D 资产。实验结果表明，使用 Habitat-GS 训练的智能体在跨领域泛化能力上表现更强，能够有效应对人类存在的场景。

papers.title: Self-Adversarial One Step Generation via Condition Shifting
papers.abstract: Abstract APEx enables efficient one-step text-to-image synthesis by eliminating adversarial training through endogenous gradient estimation from flow models, achieving superior quality and speed compared to existing methods.  AI-generated summary The push for efficient text to image synthesis has moved the field toward one step sampling, yet existing methods still face a three way tradeoff among fidelity, inference speed, and training efficiency. Approaches that rely on external discriminators can sharpen one step performance, but they often introduce training instability, high GPU memory overhead, and slow convergence, which complicates scaling and parameter efficient tuning. In contrast, regression based distillation and consistency objectives are easier to optimize, but they typically lose fine details when constrained to a single step. We present APEX, built on a key theoretical insight: adversarial correction signals can be extracted endogenously from a flow model through condition shifting. Using a transformation creates a shifted condition branch whose velocity field serves as an independent estimator of the model's current generation distribution, yielding a gradient that is provably GAN aligned, replacing the sample dependent discriminator terms that cause gradient vanishing. This discriminator free design is architecture preserving, making APEX a plug and play framework compatible with both full parameter and LoRA based tuning. Empirically, our 0.6B model surpasses FLUX-Schnell 12B (20times more parameters) in one step quality. With LoRA tuning on Qwen-Image 20B, APEX reaches a GenEval score of 0.89 at NFE=1 in 6 hours, surpassing the original 50-step teacher (0.87) and providing a 15.33times inference speedup. Code is available https://github.com/LINs-lab/APEX.

ru.title: Дискриминатор-свободный синтез изображений через эндогенные потоки
ru.desc: APEx представляет новый метод для быстрого генерирования изображений из текста в один шаг, который исключает использование дискриминатора благодаря эндогенной оценке градиентов из flow-моделей. Ключевая идея заключается в извлечении сигналов коррекции непосредственно из самой модели через трансформацию условия, что избегает проблем нестабильности обучения и утечки градиентов. Метод совместим как с полным обучением параметров, так и с LoRA-адаптацией, обеспечивая архитектурную универсальность. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что компактная модель на 0.6B параметров превосходит FLUX-Schnell с 12B параметров по качеству при одношаговом синтезе и достигает 15-кратного ускорения инференса.

en.title: APEx: Revolutionizing Text-to-Image Synthesis with Efficiency and Quality
en.desc: APEx introduces a novel approach to text-to-image synthesis by eliminating the need for adversarial training, which often complicates the training process. It utilizes endogenous gradient estimation from flow models to achieve high-quality image generation in a single step. This method addresses the common trade-offs in fidelity, speed, and training efficiency by providing a discriminator-free design that maintains architectural integrity. Empirical results show that APEx outperforms larger models in both quality and inference speed, making it a significant advancement in the field.

zh.title: APEx：高效的一步文本到图像合成
zh.desc: APEx是一种高效的一步文本到图像合成方法，它通过流模型的内生梯度估计消除了对抗训练，从而在质量和速度上超越了现有方法。传统方法在保真度、推理速度和训练效率之间存在三重权衡，而APEx通过条件转移提取对抗校正信号，避免了外部判别器带来的不稳定性和高内存开销。APEx的设计不依赖于样本相关的判别器，保持了架构的兼容性，使其能够与全参数和LoRA调优相结合。实验结果表明，APEx在一步生成质量上超过了参数量更大的FLUX-Schnell，并在推理速度上实现了显著提升。

papers.title: The Blind Spot of Agent Safety: How Benign User Instructions Expose Critical Vulnerabilities in Computer-Use Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Computer-use agents face significant safety vulnerabilities under unintended attack conditions where benign instructions lead to harmful outcomes through contextual or execution-based risks, with attack success rates exceeding 90% even in safety-aligned models.  AI-generated summary Computer-use agents (CUAs) can now autonomously complete complex tasks in real digital environments, but when misled, they can also be used to automate harmful actions programmatically. Existing safety evaluations largely target explicit threats such as misuse and prompt injection, but overlook a subtle yet critical setting where user instructions are entirely benign and harm arises from the task context or execution outcome. We introduce OS-BLIND, a benchmark that evaluates CUAs under unintended attack conditions, comprising 300 human-crafted tasks across 12 categories, 8 applications, and 2 threat clusters: environment-embedded threats and agent-initiated harms. Our evaluation on frontier models and agentic frameworks reveals that most CUAs exceed 90% attack success rate (ASR), and even the safety-aligned Claude 4.5 Sonnet reaches 73.0% ASR. More interestingly, this vulnerability becomes even more severe, with ASR rising from 73.0% to 92.7% when Claude 4.5 Sonnet is deployed in multi-agent systems. Our analysis further shows that existing safety defenses provide limited protection when user instructions are benign. Safety alignment primarily activates within the first few steps and rarely re-engages during subsequent execution. In multi-agent systems, decomposed subtasks obscure the harmful intent from the model, causing safety-aligned models to fail. We will release our OS-BLIND to encourage the broader research community to further investigate and address these safety challenges.

ru.title: Скрытые угрозы в системах автономных агентов
ru.desc: Статья исследует уязвимости агентов, способных взаимодействовать с операционными системами, в условиях непреднамеренных атак, где безобидные инструкции приводят к вредоносным результатам. Авторы представили бенчмарк OS-BLIND с 300 задачами для оценки стойкости таких агентов к контекстным и связанным с исполнением угрозам. Тестирование показало, что даже защищённые модели, такие как Claude 4.5 Sonnet, демонстрируют более 70% уровня успешности атак, который возрастает до 92.7% в мультиагентных системах. Анализ выявил, что существующие методы защиты неэффективны при безвредных инструкциях, так как механизмы безопасности срабатывают только на начальных этапах выполнения задачи.

en.title: Uncovering Hidden Risks in AI: The OS-BLIND Benchmark
en.desc: This paper discusses the vulnerabilities of computer-use agents (CUAs) when they are given benign instructions that can lead to harmful outcomes due to contextual or execution risks. The authors introduce a new benchmark called OS-BLIND, which evaluates CUAs under unintended attack conditions, revealing that many models have high attack success rates, even those designed with safety in mind. The study shows that existing safety measures are often ineffective when user instructions are harmless, particularly in multi-agent systems where the complexity increases the risk. The findings highlight the need for improved safety evaluations and defenses in AI systems to better protect against these subtle threats.

zh.title: 无害指令下的安全隐患
zh.desc: 计算机使用代理（CUAs）在意外攻击条件下面临重大安全漏洞，即使在安全对齐模型中，攻击成功率也超过90%。这些代理可以在真实数字环境中自主完成复杂任务，但在被误导时也可能被用来自动化有害行为。现有的安全评估主要针对明显的威胁，如误用和提示注入，而忽视了用户指令完全无害但仍可能导致伤害的微妙情境。我们提出了OS-BLIND基准，评估CUAs在意外攻击条件下的表现，结果显示大多数CUAs的攻击成功率极高，尤其是在多代理系统中更为严重。

papers.title: Rethinking the Diffusion Model from a Langevin Perspective
papers.abstract: Abstract The article provides a unified Langevin perspective on diffusion models, clarifying their theoretical foundations and connections between different mathematical formulations.  AI-generated summary Diffusion models are often introduced from multiple perspectives, such as VAEs, score matching, or flow matching, accompanied by dense and technically demanding mathematics that can be difficult for beginners to grasp. One classic question is: how does the reverse process invert the forward process to generate data from pure noise? This article systematically organizes the diffusion model from a fresh Langevin perspective, offering a simpler, clearer, and more intuitive answer. We also address the following questions: how can ODE-based and SDE-based diffusion models be unified under a single framework? Why are diffusion models theoretically superior to ordinary VAEs? Why is flow matching not fundamentally simpler than denoising or score matching, but equivalent under maximum-likelihood? We demonstrate that the Langevin perspective offers clear and straightforward answers to these questions, bridging existing interpretations of diffusion models, showing how different formulations can be converted into one another within a common framework, and offering pedagogical value for both learners and experienced researchers seeking deeper intuition.
affiliations: Department of Mathematics, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Уравнения Ланжевена как единая основа для всех диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: Статья предлагает единую перспективу на диффузионные модели через призму уравнений Ланжевена, упрощая их теоретическое описание. Авторы показывают, как различные подходы — от вариационных автокодировщиков до matching потоков — могут быть объединены в одну математическую рамку. Они объясняют, почему обратный процесс успешно инвертирует прямой процесс, превращая шум в данные, и демонстрируют эквивалентность ODE и SDE формулировок диффузии. Такая унифицированная точка зрения помогает понять, почему диффузионные модели превосходят простые VAE и делает обучение более интуитивным для специалистов любого уровня.

en.title: Unifying Diffusion Models with Langevin Dynamics
en.desc: This paper presents a unified view of diffusion models through the lens of Langevin dynamics, simplifying their complex mathematical foundations. It clarifies how the reverse diffusion process can effectively generate data from noise, addressing common questions in the field. The authors demonstrate that both ODE-based and SDE-based diffusion models can be integrated into a single framework, highlighting their theoretical advantages over traditional Variational Autoencoders (VAEs). By providing a clearer understanding of flow matching, denoising, and score matching, this work serves as a valuable resource for both newcomers and seasoned researchers in machine learning.

zh.title: 统一视角下的扩散模型解析
zh.desc: 本文从统一的Langevin视角出发，阐明了扩散模型的理论基础及其不同数学形式之间的联系。扩散模型通常通过多种视角引入，涉及复杂的数学内容，初学者难以理解。文章系统地整理了扩散模型，提供了更简单、清晰和直观的解释，特别是关于如何通过反向过程从纯噪声生成数据的问题。我们还探讨了ODE和SDE基础的扩散模型如何在一个框架下统一，以及扩散模型为何在理论上优于普通的变分自编码器（VAE）。

papers.title: LARY: A Latent Action Representation Yielding Benchmark for Generalizable Vision-to-Action Alignment
papers.abstract: Abstract General visual foundation models trained without action supervision outperform specialized embodied models and demonstrate superior alignment between visual and physical action spaces compared to pixel-based approaches.  AI-generated summary While the shortage of explicit action data limits Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, human action videos offer a scalable yet unlabeled data source. A critical challenge in utilizing large-scale human video datasets lies in transforming visual signals into ontology-independent representations, known as latent actions. However, the capacity of latent action representation to derive robust control from visual observations has yet to be rigorously evaluated. We introduce the Latent Action Representation Yielding (LARY) Benchmark, a unified framework for evaluating latent action representations on both high-level semantic actions (what to do) and low-level robotic control (how to do). The comprehensively curated dataset encompasses over one million videos (1,000 hours) spanning 151 action categories, alongside 620K image pairs and 595K motion trajectories across diverse embodiments and environments. Our experiments reveal two crucial insights: (i) General visual foundation models, trained without any action supervision, consistently outperform specialized embodied latent action models. (ii) Latent-based visual space is fundamentally better aligned to physical action space than pixel-based space. These results suggest that general visual representations inherently encode action-relevant knowledge for physical control, and that semantic-level abstraction serves as a fundamentally more effective pathway from vision to action than pixel-level reconstruction.

ru.title: Как видение без действий учит робота управлять собой
ru.desc: Работа исследует проблему использования больших наборов видео с человеческими действиями для обучения моделей vision-language-action без явной разметки действий. Авторы предлагают бенчмарк LARY для оценки представлений скрытых действий (latent actions) — онтологически независимых визуальных представлений, которые можно трансформировать в команды управления. Ключевой результат: общие визуальные foundation модели, обученные без какой-либо разметки действий, превосходят специализированные воплощённые модели и лучше согласуются с физическим пространством действий. Исследование показывает, что семантическая абстракция визуальной информации является более эффективным путём от зрения к действию, чем прямая реконструкция пикселей.

en.title: Unlocking Action Understanding with General Visual Models
en.desc: This paper discusses the effectiveness of general visual foundation models in understanding and executing actions without needing specific action data. It highlights the introduction of the Latent Action Representation Yielding (LARY) Benchmark, which evaluates how well visual signals can be transformed into actionable representations. The study shows that these general models outperform specialized models that rely on explicit action supervision. Additionally, it finds that representations based on latent actions are better aligned with physical actions than traditional pixel-based methods, suggesting a more efficient way to connect visual input to real-world actions.

zh.title: 通用视觉模型超越专门模型的力量
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了在没有动作监督的情况下训练的通用视觉基础模型，其性能优于专门的具身模型，并且在视觉与物理动作空间之间的对齐效果更佳。我们提出了潜在动作表示收益基准（LARY Benchmark），用于评估潜在动作表示在高层语义动作和低层机器人控制上的表现。研究表明，通用视觉模型能够有效地从视觉观察中提取与动作相关的知识，而语义层次的抽象比像素级重建更有效地连接视觉与动作。我们的数据集包含超过一百万个视频，涵盖151个动作类别，为进一步研究提供了丰富的资源。

papers.title: You Only Judge Once: Multi-response Reward Modeling in a Single Forward Pass
papers.abstract: Abstract A multimodal reward model evaluates multiple responses simultaneously through concatenated input and cross-entropy scoring, achieving faster training and superior performance in open-ended generation tasks compared to traditional single-response approaches.  AI-generated summary We present a discriminative multimodal reward model that scores all candidate responses in a single forward pass. Conventional discriminative reward models evaluate each response independently, requiring multiple forward passes, one for each potential response. Our approach concatenates multiple responses with separator tokens and applies cross-entropy over their scalar scores, enabling direct comparative reasoning and efficient N-way preference learning. The multi-response design also yields up to Ntimes wall-clock speedup and FLOPs reduction over conventional single-response scoring. To enable N-way reward evaluation beyond existing pairwise benchmarks, we construct two new benchmarks: (1) MR^2Bench-Image contains human-annotated rankings over responses from 8 diverse models; (2) MR^2Bench-Video is a large-scale video-based reward benchmark derived from 94K crowdsourced pairwise human judgments over video question-answering spanning 19 models, denoised via preference graph ensemble. Both benchmarks provide 4-response evaluation variants sampled from the full rankings. Built on a 4B vision-language backbone with LoRA fine-tuning and a lightweight MLP value head, our model achieves state-of-the-art results on six multimodal reward benchmarks, including MR^2Bench-Image, MR^2Bench-Video, and four other existing benchmarks. Our model outperforms existing larger generative and discriminative reward models. We further demonstrate that our reward model, when used in reinforcement learning with GRPO, produces improved policy models that maintain performance across standard multimodal benchmarks while substantially improving open-ended generation quality, outperforming a single-response discriminative reward model (RM) baseline by a large margin in both training stability and open-ended generation quality.
affiliations: Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering, University of Washington

ru.title: Многоответная модель вознаграждения для эффективной оценки и обучения мультимодальных систем
ru.desc: В статье предлагается дискриминативная мультимодальная модель вознаграждения, которая оценивает несколько ответов одновременно в одном проходе нейронной сети. В отличие от традиционных подходов, требующих отдельных прямых проходов для каждого ответа, новый метод конкатенирует несколько ответов и применяет кросс-энтропийную функцию потерь для прямого сравнительного анализа. Авторы создали два новых датасета для оценки N-way предпочтений с человеческими аннотациями и показали, что их модель достигает передовых результатов на нескольких бенчмарках. Используя полученную модель вознаграждения в обучении с подкреплением, они получили улучшенные модели политик с лучшим качеством генерации открытого формата.

en.title: Faster and Smarter: Multimodal Reward Evaluation for Open-Ended Generation
en.desc: This paper introduces a multimodal reward model that evaluates multiple responses at once, making the training process faster and more efficient. Unlike traditional models that assess each response separately, this approach uses concatenated inputs and cross-entropy scoring to compare responses directly. The model achieves significant speed improvements and reduces computational load by processing multiple responses in a single forward pass. Additionally, it establishes new benchmarks for evaluating responses in both image and video contexts, demonstrating superior performance in open-ended generation tasks compared to existing models.

zh.title: 多模态奖励模型：提升生成质量与训练效率
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种多模态奖励模型，可以同时评估多个响应，使用连接输入和交叉熵评分的方法。这种方法相比传统的单响应评估，训练速度更快，性能更优。通过将多个响应连接在一起并使用分隔符，模型能够进行直接的比较推理，提升了学习效率。该模型在多个多模态奖励基准测试中表现出色，尤其是在开放式生成任务中，显著提高了生成质量和训练稳定性。

papers.title: Generative Refinement Networks for Visual Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract Generative Refinement Networks introduce a novel visual synthesis approach that combines hierarchical binary quantization with adaptive refinement mechanisms to improve computational efficiency and visual quality in image generation.  AI-generated summary While diffusion models dominate the field of visual generation, they are computationally inefficient, applying a uniform computational effort regardless of different complexity. In contrast, autoregressive (AR) models are inherently complexity-aware, as evidenced by their variable likelihoods, but are often hindered by lossy discrete tokenization and error accumulation. In this work, we introduce Generative Refinement Networks (GRN), a next-generation visual synthesis paradigm to address these issues. At its core, GRN addresses the discrete tokenization bottleneck through a theoretically near-lossless Hierarchical Binary Quantization (HBQ), achieving a reconstruction quality comparable to continuous counterparts. Built upon HBQ's latent space, GRN fundamentally upgrades AR generation with a global refinement mechanism that progressively perfects and corrects artworks -- like a human artist painting. Besides, GRN integrates an entropy-guided sampling strategy, enabling complexity-aware, adaptive-step generation without compromising visual quality. On the ImageNet benchmark, GRN establishes new records in image reconstruction (0.56 rFID) and class-conditional image generation (1.81 gFID). We also scale GRN to more challenging text-to-image and text-to-video generation, delivering superior performance on an equivalent scale. We release all models and code to foster further research on GRN.

ru.title: Умная генерация с адаптивным уточнением для качественного синтеза изображений
ru.desc: В работе представлены Generative Refinement Networks (GRN) — новая парадигма для синтеза изображений, которая комбинирует иерархическую двоичную квантизацию с механизмом адаптивного уточнения. Подход решает проблему потери информации при дискретизации токенов и преодолевает недостатки авторегрессионных моделей, используя теоретически практически бесстратных кодирование. GRN интегрирует глобальный механизм уточнения, который итеративно улучшает генерируемые изображения подобно работе художника, и стратегию семплирования, управляемую энтропией для адаптивной генерации. Модель устанавливает новые рекорды на бенчмарке ImageNet и демонстрирует превосходные результаты в задачах text-to-image и text-to-video генерации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Image Generation with Generative Refinement Networks
en.desc: Generative Refinement Networks (GRN) present a new method for generating images that enhances both efficiency and quality. By using Hierarchical Binary Quantization (HBQ), GRN minimizes the loss typically associated with discrete tokenization, allowing for high-quality image reconstruction. The model incorporates a global refinement mechanism that iteratively improves generated images, similar to how an artist refines their work. Additionally, GRN employs an entropy-guided sampling strategy to adaptively manage complexity during generation, achieving state-of-the-art results on benchmarks like ImageNet.

zh.title: 生成精炼网络：高效与高质的图像合成新方法
zh.desc: 生成精炼网络（GRN）是一种新型的视觉合成方法，结合了分层二进制量化和自适应精炼机制，以提高图像生成的计算效率和视觉质量。与扩散模型相比，GRN能够更有效地处理不同复杂度的图像生成任务，避免了均匀计算带来的低效。GRN通过近乎无损的分层二进制量化技术，解决了离散化瓶颈，重建质量接近连续模型。该网络还引入了基于熵的采样策略，实现了复杂度感知的自适应生成，确保了视觉质量的提升。

papers.title: Accelerating Speculative Decoding with Block Diffusion Draft Trees
papers.abstract: Abstract DDTree enhances speculative decoding by constructing draft trees from block diffusion drafter distributions and efficiently verifying multiple trajectories in parallel.  AI-generated summary Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive language models by using a lightweight drafter to propose multiple future tokens, which the target model then verifies in parallel. DFlash shows that a block diffusion drafter can generate an entire draft block in a single forward pass and achieve state-of-the-art speculative decoding performance, outperforming strong autoregressive drafters such as EAGLE-3. Vanilla DFlash, however, still verifies only a single drafted trajectory per round, potentially limiting its acceptance length. We introduce DDTree (Diffusion Draft Tree), a method that constructs a draft tree directly from the per-position distributions of a block diffusion drafter. Under a fixed node budget, DDTree uses a simple best-first heap algorithm to select the continuations that are most likely to match the target model according to a surrogate defined by the draft model's output. The resulting tree is verified efficiently in a single target model forward pass using an ancestor-only attention mask. Because DDTree builds on DFlash, a leading draft model for speculative decoding, these gains place DDTree among the leading approaches to speculative decoding.

ru.title: Ускорение языковых моделей через дерево вероятностных путей
ru.desc: DDTree — это метод ускорения автreggressive языковых моделей, который строит дерево черновых последовательностей из распределений вероятностей блочного диффузионного драфтера. Вместо проверки одной последовательности за раз, метод использует жадный алгоритм поиска в ширину для выбора наиболее вероятных продолжений и создаёт дерево кандидатов под фиксированный бюджет узлов. Все варианты в дереве проверяются параллельно целевой моделью за один проход с использованием специальной маски внимания, что позволяет значительно увеличить длину принятых токенов. Метод основан на DFlash — передовом драфтере с блочной диффузией, и достигает лучших результатов среди современных подходов спекулятивного декодирования.

en.title: DDTree: Enhancing Speculative Decoding with Draft Trees
en.desc: DDTree is a novel method that improves speculative decoding in language models by creating draft trees from block diffusion drafter distributions. It allows for the verification of multiple token trajectories simultaneously, enhancing the efficiency of the decoding process. By utilizing a best-first heap algorithm, DDTree selects the most promising continuations based on the draft model's output, optimizing the selection process. This approach not only builds on the strengths of the DFlash model but also achieves state-of-the-art performance in speculative decoding tasks.

zh.title: DDTree：推测解码的新突破
zh.desc: DDTree是一种增强推测解码的方法，它通过从块扩散草拟器的分布中构建草拟树来实现，并能高效地并行验证多个轨迹。推测解码通过使用轻量级草拟器提出多个未来的标记，从而加速自回归语言模型的生成。DDTree利用简单的最佳优先堆算法，在固定的节点预算下选择最有可能与目标模型匹配的延续，并通过祖先注意力掩码在单次前向传递中高效验证生成的树。由于DDTree基于DFlash这一领先的草拟模型，因此在推测解码性能上具有显著的提升。

papers.title: Masked by Consensus: Disentangling Privileged Knowledge in LLM Correctness
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models do not demonstrate superior self-awareness of answer correctness compared to external models, though they show domain-specific advantages in factual knowledge tasks when models disagree on predictions.  AI-generated summary Humans use introspection to evaluate their understanding through private internal states inaccessible to external observers. We investigate whether large language models possess similar privileged knowledge about answer correctness, information unavailable through external observation. We train correctness classifiers on question representations from both a model's own hidden states and external models, testing whether self-representations provide a performance advantage. On standard evaluation, we find no advantage: self-probes perform comparably to peer-model probes. We hypothesize this is due to high inter-model agreement of answer correctness. To isolate genuine privileged knowledge, we evaluate on disagreement subsets, where models produce conflicting predictions. Here, we discover domain-specific privileged knowledge: self-representations consistently outperform peer representations in factual knowledge tasks, but show no advantage in math reasoning. We further localize this domain asymmetry across model layers, finding that the factual advantage emerges progressively from early-to-mid layers onward, consistent with model-specific memory retrieval, while math reasoning shows no consistent advantage at any depth.

ru.title: Привилегированное знание LLM скрыто в фактических знаниях, а не в математическом рассуждении
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется вопрос о том, обладают ли большие языковые модели привилегированным знанием о корректности своих ответов через анализ скрытых состояний, недоступных внешним наблюдателям. Авторы обучают классификаторы корректности на представлениях вопросов из собственных скрытых состояний модели и внешних моделей, сравнивая их производительность. На стандартных наборах данных преимущества самопроверки не обнаружено, однако при анализе случаев, когда модели дают противоречивые предсказания, выявлено специфичное для предметной области привилегированное знание: самопредставления превосходят внешние представления в задачах фактических знаний, но не показывают преимущества в математическом рассуждении. Дополнительный анализ слоёв модели показывает, что преимущество в фактических знаниях постепенно появляется с ранних-средних слоёв, что соответствует специфичному для модели извлечению информации из памяти.

en.title: Self-Awareness in Language Models: A Limited Advantage
en.desc: This paper explores whether large language models (LLMs) have a better understanding of their own answer correctness compared to external models. The researchers trained classifiers to assess the correctness of answers based on both the LLM's internal states and those of other models. They found that LLMs do not have a significant advantage in self-awareness of correctness, as their self-evaluations are similar to those of peer models. However, in cases where models disagreed on answers, LLMs showed superior performance in factual knowledge tasks, indicating that they possess some domain-specific privileged knowledge.

zh.title: 大型语言模型的自我意识与外部模型的比较
zh.desc: 这篇论文研究了大型语言模型在回答正确性方面的自我意识。尽管这些模型在特定领域的知识任务中表现出优势，但与外部模型相比，它们在评估答案正确性时并没有显示出更好的自我意识。研究发现，在模型之间存在一致性时，自我表示与同伴模型的表示在性能上没有显著差异。然而，在模型预测不一致的情况下，自我表示在事实知识任务中表现出优势，但在数学推理方面没有明显的优势。

papers.title: Lightning OPD: Efficient Post-Training for Large Reasoning Models with Offline On-Policy Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract Lightning OPD enables efficient offline on-policy distillation for large language models by enforcing teacher consistency and eliminating the need for live teacher inference servers.  AI-generated summary On-policy distillation (OPD) has emerged as an efficient post-training paradigm for large language models. However, standard OPD requires a live teacher inference server throughout training, resulting in substantial infrastructure overhead. In this work, we investigate whether on-policy distillation can be performed offline. A natural approach is to precompute teacher log-probabilities once over SFT rollouts and reuse them during training. In practice, however, this offline variant fails to reliably match the performance of standard OPD. To understand this discrepancy, we identify a previously overlooked condition that is critical for any OPD pipeline, which we term teacher consistency. This condition requires that the same teacher model be used for both supervised fine-tuning and OPD. We show that violating teacher consistency introduces an irreducible gradient bias, causing both offline and online OPD to converge to a suboptimal fixed point regardless of training duration. Building on this insight, we propose Lightning OPD, an offline on-policy distillation framework that enforces teacher consistency by precomputing teacher log-probabilities over SFT rollouts. This design eliminates the need for a live teacher server entirely. We further show that, under teacher consistency, Lightning OPD shares the same optimum as standard OPD, with bounded gradient discrepancy and an implicit regularization effect that helps prevent policy drift. Extensive experiments on mathematical reasoning and code generation demonstrate that Lightning OPD achieves state-of-the-art performance with significantly improved efficiency. Starting from an SFT-initialized Qwen3-8B-Base model, Lightning OPD reaches 69.9% on AIME 2024 in just 30 GPU hours, achieving a 4.0x speedup over standard OPD and substantially lowering the barrier to entry for academic research on LLM post-training.

ru.title: Молниеносная дистилляция без учительского сервера
ru.desc: В статье представлен метод Lightning OPD для эффективной дистилляции больших языковых моделей в режиме офлайн, который устраняет необходимость в постоянно работающем учительском сервере. Авторы выявили критическое условие учительской консистентности, которое требует использования одной и той же модели-учителя как для supervised fine-tuning, так и для on-policy distillation. Нарушение этого условия приводит к необратимому смещению градиентов и субоптимальной сходимости. Предложенный метод достигает производительности стандартного OPD при значительном ускорении обучения и снижении вычислительных затрат.

en.title: Lightning OPD: Efficient Offline Distillation with Teacher Consistency
en.desc: This paper introduces Lightning OPD, a method for offline on-policy distillation (OPD) of large language models that enhances efficiency by ensuring teacher consistency. Traditional OPD relies on a live teacher inference server, which can be resource-intensive, but Lightning OPD eliminates this need by precomputing teacher log-probabilities from supervised fine-tuning (SFT) rollouts. The authors identify that maintaining the same teacher model throughout the training process is crucial to avoid gradient bias and achieve optimal performance. Through extensive experiments, Lightning OPD demonstrates significant improvements in efficiency and performance, achieving state-of-the-art results in mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks.

zh.title: Lightning OPD：高效的离线策略蒸馏方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为Lightning OPD的离线策略蒸馏方法，旨在提高大型语言模型的训练效率。传统的在线策略蒸馏需要实时教师推理服务器，导致基础设施开销大。研究发现，教师一致性是确保蒸馏效果的重要条件，违反这一条件会导致不可减少的梯度偏差。通过预计算教师的对数概率，Lightning OPD能够在不需要实时教师服务器的情况下，达到与标准在线策略蒸馏相同的最优性能，同时显著提高训练效率。

papers.title: GlotOCR Bench: OCR Models Still Struggle Beyond a Handful of Unicode Scripts
papers.abstract: Abstract Vision-language models show limited generalization in OCR across diverse scripts, with performance closely tied to pretraining coverage and struggling with unfamiliar writing systems.  AI-generated summary Optical character recognition (OCR) has advanced rapidly with the rise of vision-language models, yet evaluation has remained concentrated on a small cluster of high- and mid-resource scripts. We introduce GlotOCR Bench, a comprehensive benchmark evaluating OCR generalization across 100+ Unicode scripts. Our benchmark comprises clean and degraded image variants rendered from real multilingual texts. Images are rendered using fonts from the Google Fonts repository, shaped with HarfBuzz and rasterized with FreeType, supporting both LTR and RTL scripts. Samples of rendered images were manually reviewed to verify correct rendering across all scripts. We evaluate a broad suite of open-weight and proprietary vision-language models and find that most perform well on fewer than ten scripts, and even the strongest frontier models fail to generalize beyond thirty scripts. Performance broadly tracks script-level pretraining coverage, suggesting that current OCR systems rely on language model pretraining as much as on visual recognition. Models confronted with unfamiliar scripts either produce random noise or hallucinate characters from similar scripts they already know. We release the benchmark and pipeline for reproducibility. Pipeline Code: https://github.com/cisnlp/glotocr-bench, Benchmark: https://hf.co/datasets/cis-lmu/glotocr-bench.

ru.title: OCR моделям нужна обучающая выборка каждой письменности
ru.desc: В работе представлена GlotOCR Bench — комплексный бенчмарк для оценки способности моделей компьютерного зрения к распознаванию текста (OCR) на 100+ различных письменностях. Исследователи протестировали множество современных vision-language моделей и обнаружили, что большинство из них хорошо работают менее чем на десяти письменностях, а даже самые продвинутые модели не обобщаются на более чем тридцать письменностях. Выяснилось, что качество OCR напрямую зависит от объёма данных конкретной письменности в обучающем наборе модели, а не только от способности визуального распознавания. Когда модели встречают незнакомую письменность, они либо генерируют случайный шум, либо галлюцинируют символы из похожих письменностей, на которых были предварительно обучены.

en.title: Enhancing OCR Generalization Across Diverse Scripts with GlotOCR Bench
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of vision-language models in optical character recognition (OCR) when dealing with various writing systems. It introduces GlotOCR Bench, a new benchmark that tests OCR performance across over 100 Unicode scripts, including both clean and degraded image versions. The study reveals that most models excel with only a few scripts and struggle significantly with unfamiliar ones, often producing incorrect outputs. The findings indicate that the effectiveness of OCR systems is heavily influenced by the pretraining coverage of the language models used.

zh.title: 提升OCR泛化能力的基准测试
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了GlotOCR Bench，这是一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估光学字符识别（OCR）在100多种Unicode脚本中的泛化能力。研究发现，现有的视觉语言模型在处理多种脚本时表现有限，尤其是在不熟悉的书写系统上。大多数模型在少于十种脚本上表现良好，而即使是最强的模型也难以超越三十种脚本的泛化能力。结果表明，当前的OCR系统在很大程度上依赖于语言模型的预训练，而不仅仅是视觉识别。

papers.title: Do Thought Streams Matter? Evaluating Reasoning in Gemini Vision-Language Models for Video Scene Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract Research examines how internal reasoning traces affect video scene understanding in vision-language models, revealing that quality improvements from extended reasoning plateau quickly and that different model variants produce distinct reasoning patterns.  AI-generated summary We benchmark how internal reasoning traces, which we call thought streams, affect video scene understanding in vision-language models.
