Using four configurations of Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash and Flash Lite across scenes extracted from 100 hours of video, we ask three questions: does more thinking lead to better outputs, where do the gains stop, and what do these models actually think about? We introduce three evaluation metrics. Contentfulness measures how much of the thought stream is useful scene content versus meta-commentary. Thought-Final Coverage measures how faithfully the thought stream translates into the final output. Dominant Entity Analysis identifies which subjects, actions, and settings the model focuses on. GPT-5 serves as an independent judge. We find that quality gains from additional thinking plateau quickly, with most improvement occurring in the first few hundred tokens. Flash Lite offers the best balance between quality and token usage. Tight reasoning budgets cause the model to add content in the final output that it never reasoned about, a form of compression-step hallucination. Despite being different model tiers, Flash and Flash Lite produce similar thought streams, though they differ in style: Flash discusses its reasoning process, while Lite focuses on describing the scene.

ru.title: Когда много думать - не значит думать лучше: платили размышлений в видеоанализе
ru.desc: В работе исследуется влияние внутренних цепочек рассуждений на понимание видеосцен в мультимодальных моделях. Авторы вводят три метрики для оценки качества этих рассуждений: информативность, соответствие между промежуточными рассуждениями и финальным выводом, а также анализ доминирующих объектов. Ключевое открытие заключается в том, что улучшение качества от дополнительных вычислений стремительно затухает, причём основной прирост производительности достигается в первых сотнях токенов. Исследование показывает, что ограничение вычислительного бюджета приводит к галлюцинациям, когда модель добавляет информацию в финальный ответ, которую она никогда не обсуждала в цепочке рассуждений.

en.title: Unlocking Video Understanding: The Power of Thought Streams in AI
en.desc: This research investigates how the internal reasoning processes, referred to as thought streams, influence the understanding of video scenes in vision-language models. The study evaluates four configurations of Google's Gemini 2.5 models using various metrics to assess the effectiveness of reasoning in generating outputs. It finds that while additional reasoning can improve quality, the benefits diminish quickly, with most enhancements occurring within the first few hundred tokens. The results indicate that different model variants exhibit unique reasoning patterns, with Flash providing a more detailed reasoning process compared to Flash Lite, which emphasizes scene description.

zh.title: 推理轨迹与视频理解的关系
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了内部推理轨迹如何影响视觉语言模型中的视频场景理解。我们发现，延长推理时间带来的质量提升很快达到饱和，不同模型变体产生不同的推理模式。通过对谷歌的Gemini 2.5 Flash和Flash Lite的四种配置进行评估，我们引入了三种评估指标来分析推理过程的有效性。结果表明，Flash Lite在质量和令牌使用之间提供了最佳平衡，而紧凑的推理预算可能导致模型在最终输出中添加未曾推理的内容。

papers.title: Parcae: Scaling Laws For Stable Looped Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Looped architectures can improve model quality by increasing computational complexity, but suffer from instability; a new stable looped architecture called Parcae addresses these issues through constrained spectral norms and demonstrates superior scaling properties.  AI-generated summary Traditional fixed-depth architectures scale quality by increasing training FLOPs, typically through increased parameterization, at the expense of a higher memory footprint, or data. A potential alternative is looped architectures, which instead increase FLOPs by sending activations through a block of layers in a loop. While promising, existing recipes for training looped architectures can be unstable, suffering from residual explosion and loss spikes. We address these challenges by recasting looping as a nonlinear time-variant dynamical system over the residual stream. Via a linear approximation to this system, we find that instability occurs in existing looped architectures as a result of large spectral norms in their injection parameters. To address these instability issues, we propose Parcae, a novel stable, looped architecture that constrains the spectral norm of the injection parameters via discretization of a negative diagonal parameterization. As a result, Parcae achieves up to 6.3% lower validation perplexity over prior large-scale looped models. Using our stable looped architecture, we investigate the scaling properties of looping as a medium to improve quality by increasing FLOPs in training and test-time. For training, we derive predictable power laws to scale FLOPs while keeping parameter count fixed. Our initial scaling laws suggest that looping and data should be increased in tandem, given a fixed FLOP budget. At test-time, we find that Parcae can use looping to scale compute, following a predictable, saturating exponential decay. When scaled up to 1.3B parameters, we find that Parcae improves CORE and Core-Extended quality by 2.99 and 1.18 points when compared to strong Transformer baselines under a fixed parameter and data budget, achieving a relative quality of up to 87.5% a Transformer twice the size.

ru.title: Стабильные циклические архитектуры через контроль спектральных норм
ru.desc: В статье представлена новая архитектура Parcae, которая использует циклические слои для повышения качества модели без значительного увеличения числа параметров. Авторы анализируют циклические архитектуры как нелинейные динамические системы и выявляют, что нестабильность возникает из-за больших спектральных норм в параметрах инъекции. Они предлагают решение через констраинение спектральной нормы с помощью специальной параметризации, что обеспечивает стабильное обучение. Parcae демонстрирует улучшенные масштабируемые свойства как при обучении, так и при инференсе, достигая качества трансформера в два раза большего размера при том же бюджете параметров.

en.title: Parcae: Stability and Quality in Looped Architectures
en.desc: The paper introduces Parcae, a new stable looped architecture designed to enhance model quality while addressing the instability issues commonly associated with looped architectures. By constraining the spectral norms of injection parameters, Parcae mitigates problems like residual explosion and loss spikes that hinder training. The authors demonstrate that Parcae achieves better performance, with lower validation perplexity compared to previous models, while maintaining a fixed parameter count. Additionally, the paper explores scaling properties, suggesting that increasing both looping and data can lead to improved quality within a defined computational budget.

zh.title: 稳定循环架构Parcae：提升模型质量的新方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的稳定循环架构Parcae，旨在解决现有循环架构的不稳定性问题。通过对注入参数的谱范数进行约束，Parcae能够在增加计算复杂度的同时保持模型的稳定性。研究表明，Parcae在验证困惑度上比之前的大规模循环模型降低了6.3%。此外，Parcae在训练和测试时的扩展性表现出色，能够在固定参数和数据预算下显著提高模型质量。

papers.title: Learning Versatile Humanoid Manipulation with Touch Dreaming
papers.abstract: Abstract A multimodal Transformer architecture that integrates tactile sensing with visual and proprioceptive data enables high-dexterity humanoid manipulation through contact-aware learning and predictive modeling.  AI-generated summary Humanoid robots promise general-purpose assistance, yet real-world humanoid loco-manipulation remains challenging because it requires whole-body stability, dexterous hands, and contact-aware perception under frequent contact changes. In this work, we study dexterous, contact-rich humanoid loco-manipulation. We first develop an RL-based whole-body controller that provides stable lower-body and torso execution during complex manipulation. Built on this controller, we develop a whole-body humanoid data collection system that combines VR-based teleoperation with human-to-humanoid motion mapping, enabling efficient collection of real-world demonstrations. We then propose Humanoid Transformer with Touch Dreaming (HTD), a multimodal encoder--decoder Transformer that models touch as a core modality alongside multi-view vision and proprioception. HTD is trained in a single stage with behavioral cloning augmented by touch dreaming: in addition to predicting action chunks, the policy predicts future hand-joint forces and future tactile latents, encouraging the shared Transformer trunk to learn contact-aware representations for dexterous interaction. Across five contact-rich tasks, Insert-T, Book Organization, Towel Folding, Cat Litter Scooping, and Tea Serving, HTD achieves a 90.9% relative improvement in average success rate over the stronger baseline. Ablation results further show that latent-space tactile prediction is more effective than raw tactile prediction, yielding a 30% relative gain in success rate. These results demonstrate that combining robust whole-body execution, scalable humanoid data collection, and predictive touch-centered learning enables versatile, high-dexterity humanoid manipulation in the real world. Project webpage: humanoid-touch-dream.github.io.

ru.title: Когда робот чувствует прикосновение: Трансформер для ловкого манипулирования через тактильное восприятие
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали мультимодальную архитектуру на основе Трансформера, которая интегрирует тактильные ощущения с визуальными данными и проприоцепцией для обучения гуманоидных роботов ловкому манипулированию. Система использует имитационное обучение с дополнительной предсказательной задачей — моделированием будущих тактильных латентов и сил в суставах кисти. Авторы собрали реальные демонстрации через телеоперацию в виртуальной реальности и обучили единую модель предсказывать последовательности действий и контактные состояния. На пяти сложных задачах, требующих частого контакта с объектами, предложенный подход показал значительное улучшение — 90.9% относительного прироста успеха по сравнению с базовыми методами.

en.title: Touch-Enhanced Learning for Humanoid Dexterity
en.desc: This paper presents a multimodal Transformer architecture called Humanoid Transformer with Touch Dreaming (HTD) that enhances humanoid robot manipulation by integrating tactile sensing with visual and proprioceptive data. The approach utilizes reinforcement learning to create a stable whole-body controller, allowing robots to perform complex tasks while maintaining balance and dexterity. The HTD model is trained to predict not only actions but also future tactile feedback, which helps the robot understand and adapt to contact changes during manipulation. The results show significant improvements in task success rates, demonstrating the effectiveness of combining touch-aware learning with advanced data collection techniques for real-world applications.

zh.title: 触觉驱动的高灵活度人形机器人操作
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种多模态Transformer架构，结合了触觉感知、视觉和本体感知数据，以实现高灵活度的人形机器人操作。研究中开发了一种基于强化学习的全身控制器，确保在复杂操作中下半身和躯干的稳定执行。通过虚拟现实远程操作与人类到人形机器人的运动映射，建立了高效的数据收集系统。最终，提出的触觉梦想人形Transformer（HTD）在五个接触丰富的任务中实现了90.9%的成功率提升，展示了触觉中心学习与全身执行的结合在现实世界中的应用潜力。

papers.title: VideoFlexTok: Flexible-Length Coarse-to-Fine Video Tokenization
papers.abstract: Abstract VideoFlexTok enables efficient video representation through variable-length token sequences that capture abstract information first, followed by fine-grained details, allowing for reduced computational requirements in video generation tasks.  AI-generated summary Visual tokenizers map high-dimensional raw pixels into a compressed representation for downstream modeling. Beyond compression, tokenizers dictate what information is preserved and how it is organized. A de facto standard approach to video tokenization is to represent a video as a spatiotemporal 3D grid of tokens, each capturing the corresponding local information in the original signal. This requires the downstream model that consumes the tokens, e.g., a text-to-video model, to learn to predict all low-level details "pixel-by-pixel" irrespective of the video's inherent complexity, leading to high learning complexity.   We present VideoFlexTok, which represents videos with a variable-length sequence of tokens structured in a coarse-to-fine manner -- where the first tokens (emergently) capture abstract information, such as semantics and motion, and later tokens add fine-grained details. The generative flow decoder enables realistic video reconstructions from any token count. This representation structure allows adapting the token count according to downstream needs and encoding videos longer than the baselines with the same budget.   We evaluate VideoFlexTok on class- and text-to-video generative tasks and show that it leads to more efficient training compared to 3D grid tokens, e.g., achieving comparable generation quality (gFVD and ViCLIP Score) with a 5x smaller model (1.1B vs 5.2B). Finally, we demonstrate how VideoFlexTok can enable long video generation without prohibitive computational cost by training a text-to-video model on 10-second 81-frame videos with only 672 tokens, 8x fewer than a comparable 3D grid tokenizer.
affiliations: Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Lausanne (EPFL)

ru.title: От абстрактного к деталям: переменные токены для эффективной генерации видео
ru.desc: VideoFlexTok — это новый метод токенизации видео, который представляет видео в виде переменной длины последовательности токенов, организованных от грубого к деталям. Первые токены кодируют абстрактную информацию (семантику и движение), а последующие токены добавляют мелкие детали, что позволяет моделям генерации видео эффективнее учиться предсказывать содержание. Подход использует специальный декодер обобщающего потока, который может восстанавливать реалистичные видео из любого количества токенов, адаптируя число токенов под конкретные задачи. На практике это достигает качества сравнимого с базовыми методами, используя в 5 раз меньше параметров модели и в 8 раз меньше токенов при генерации длинных видео.

en.title: Efficient Video Generation with Variable-Length Tokens
en.desc: VideoFlexTok introduces a novel method for video representation using variable-length token sequences that prioritize abstract information before detailing finer aspects. This approach reduces the computational load in video generation tasks by allowing models to focus on essential semantics and motion first, rather than processing every pixel. By employing a coarse-to-fine structure, VideoFlexTok enables the generation of high-quality videos with fewer tokens, making it adaptable to various downstream applications. The results show that it achieves similar performance to traditional methods while significantly decreasing model size and resource requirements.

zh.title: 高效视频生成的新方法：VideoFlexTok
zh.desc: VideoFlexTok 是一种高效的视频表示方法，通过可变长度的令牌序列来捕捉视频的抽象信息和细节。这种方法首先提取语义和运动等抽象信息，然后再添加细致的细节，从而减少视频生成任务的计算需求。与传统的三维网格令牌方法相比，VideoFlexTok 可以根据下游任务的需要调整令牌数量，并在相同预算下编码更长的视频。实验表明，VideoFlexTok 在生成质量上与较小的模型相比，训练效率更高，能够实现长视频生成而不增加过高的计算成本。

papers.title: LASA: Language-Agnostic Semantic Alignment at the Semantic Bottleneck for LLM Safety
papers.abstract: Abstract Language-Agnostic Semantic Alignment (LASA) addresses LLM safety gaps across languages by targeting semantic bottlenecks where representations are primarily driven by shared semantics rather than language identity, achieving significant improvements in safety performance across multiple models.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) often demonstrate strong safety performance in high-resource languages, yet exhibit severe vulnerabilities when queried in low-resource languages. We attribute this gap to a mismatch between language-agnostic semantic understanding ability and language-dominant safety alignment biased toward high-resource languages. Consistent with this hypothesis, we empirically identify the semantic bottleneck in LLMs, an intermediate layer in which the geometry of model representations is governed primarily by shared semantic content rather than language identity. Building on this observation, we propose Language-Agnostic Semantic Alignment (LASA), which anchors safety alignment directly in semantic bottlenecks. Experiments show that LASA substantially improves safety across all languages: average attack success rate (ASR) drops from 24.7% to 2.8% on LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct and remains around 3-4% across Qwen2.5 and Qwen3 Instruct models (7B-32B). Together, our analysis and method offer a representation-level perspective on LLM safety, suggesting that safety alignment requires anchoring safety understanding not in surface text, but in the model's language-agnostic semantic space.
affiliations: The Conversational AI (CoAI) group, DCST, Tsinghua University

ru.title: Безопасность через семантику: универсальная защита LLM для всех языков
ru.desc: Статья исследует проблему безопасности больших языковых моделей в низкоресурсных языках, которые уязвимы для атак в отличие от высокоресурсных языков. Авторы выявили семантическое узкое место — промежуточный слой нейросети, где представления управляются главным образом общей семантикой, а не языковой идентичностью. На основе этого наблюдения предложен метод Language-Agnostic Semantic Alignment (LASA), который направлен на выравнивание безопасности непосредственно в семантических узких местах. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение защиты от атак: показатель успеха атак снизился с 24,7% до 2,8% на различных моделях языков.

en.title: Bridging Language Gaps for Safer AI
en.desc: The paper introduces Language-Agnostic Semantic Alignment (LASA) to improve the safety of large language models (LLMs) across different languages. It identifies a critical issue where LLMs perform well in high-resource languages but struggle in low-resource ones due to a focus on language identity rather than shared semantics. By targeting semantic bottlenecks in the model's architecture, LASA aligns safety measures with the underlying semantic content instead of just the language used. Experimental results show a significant reduction in attack success rates, demonstrating that a semantic-focused approach enhances safety performance across all languages.

zh.title: 语言无关的安全对齐提升LLM安全性
zh.desc: 本文提出了语言无关的语义对齐（LASA），旨在解决大型语言模型（LLM）在不同语言中的安全性差距。研究发现，LLM在高资源语言中表现良好，但在低资源语言中存在严重的安全漏洞，这主要是由于语义理解与语言主导的安全对齐之间的不匹配。通过识别LLM中的语义瓶颈，作者提出将安全对齐直接锚定在这些语义瓶颈上，从而显著提高了所有语言的安全性表现。实验结果表明，LASA方法使得攻击成功率显著降低，展示了在模型的语言无关语义空间中进行安全对齐的重要性。

papers.title: Hierarchical SVG Tokenization: Learning Compact Visual Programs for Scalable Vector Graphics Modeling
papers.abstract: Abstract HiVG introduces a hierarchical SVG tokenization framework that improves autoregressive vector graphics generation by addressing geometric structure representation and spatial consistency issues through atomic and segment tokens, along with a novel initialization strategy and curriculum training.  AI-generated summary Recent large language models have shifted SVG generation from differentiable rendering optimization to autoregressive program synthesis. However, existing approaches still rely on generic byte-level tokenization inherited from natural language processing, which poorly reflects the geometric structure of vector graphics. Numerical coordinates are fragmented into discrete symbols, destroying spatial relationships and introducing severe token redundancy, often leading to coordinate hallucination and inefficient long-sequence generation. To address these challenges, we propose HiVG, a hierarchical SVG tokenization framework tailored for autoregressive vector graphics generation. HiVG decomposes raw SVG strings into structured atomic tokens and further compresses executable command--parameter groups into geometry-constrained segment tokens, substantially improving sequence efficiency while preserving syntactic validity. To further mitigate spatial mismatch, we introduce a Hierarchical Mean--Noise (HMN) initialization strategy that injects numerical ordering signals and semantic priors into new token embeddings. Combined with a curriculum training paradigm that progressively increases program complexity, HiVG enables more stable learning of executable SVG programs. Extensive experiments on both text-to-SVG and image-to-SVG tasks demonstrate improved generation fidelity, spatial consistency, and sequence efficiency compared with conventional tokenization schemes. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/ximinng/HiVG

ru.title: Иерархическая токенизация для автарегрессивного генерирования векторной графики
ru.desc: HiVG представляет иерархическую систему токенизации для векторной графики, которая улучшает автарегрессивное генерирование SVG путём правильного представления геометрической структуры. Вместо байт-уровневой токенизации, заимствованной из обработки естественного языка, метод использует структурированные атомарные токены и сегменты команд-параметров, сохраняя пространственные отношения. Для решения проблемы пространственного несоответствия предложена стратегия инициализации Hierarchical Mean-Noise, которая внедряет числовые сигналы порядка в эмбеддинги токенов. Комбинированный с curriculum learning подход позволяет более стабильно обучать модели генерированию корректных SVG программ с улучшенной точностью и последовательной эффективностью.

en.title: Revolutionizing SVG Generation with Hierarchical Tokenization
en.desc: HiVG is a new framework designed to improve the generation of vector graphics using autoregressive models. It introduces a hierarchical tokenization method that breaks down SVG data into atomic and segment tokens, which better represent geometric structures and maintain spatial relationships. This approach reduces token redundancy and prevents issues like coordinate hallucination, leading to more efficient long-sequence generation. Additionally, HiVG employs a novel initialization strategy and a curriculum training method to enhance the learning process, resulting in higher fidelity and consistency in generated SVG outputs.

zh.title: 层次化SVG标记化，提升图形生成效率
zh.desc: HiVG是一种层次化的SVG标记化框架，旨在改善自回归矢量图形生成中的几何结构表示和空间一致性问题。它通过原子标记和段标记的方式，将原始SVG字符串分解为结构化的标记，从而提高序列效率并保持语法有效性。为了进一步减少空间不匹配，HiVG引入了一种层次均值-噪声初始化策略，注入数值排序信号和语义先验。通过逐步增加程序复杂度的课程训练，HiVG实现了更稳定的可执行SVG程序学习。

papers.title: Domain-Specific Latent Representations Improve the Fidelity of Diffusion-Based Medical Image Super-Resolution
papers.abstract: Abstract Replacing generic VAEs with domain-specific autoencoders in latent diffusion models significantly improves medical image super-resolution quality, with reconstruction fidelity being predictable from autoencoder performance alone.  AI-generated summary Latent diffusion models for medical image super-resolution universally inherit variational autoencoders designed for natural photographs. We show that this default choice, not the diffusion architecture, is the dominant constraint on reconstruction quality. In a controlled experiment holding all other pipeline components fixed, replacing the generic Stable Diffusion VAE with MedVAE, a domain-specific autoencoder pretrained on more than 1.6 million medical images, yields +2.91 to +3.29 dB PSNR improvement across knee MRI, brain MRI, and chest X-ray (n = 1,820; Cohen's d = 1.37 to 1.86, all p < 10^{-20}, Wilcoxon signed-rank). Wavelet decomposition localises the advantage to the finest spatial frequency bands encoding anatomically relevant fine structure. Ablations across inference schedules, prediction targets, and generative architectures confirm the gap is stable within plus or minus 0.15 dB, while hallucination rates remain comparable between methods (Cohen's h < 0.02 across all datasets), establishing that reconstruction fidelity and generative hallucination are governed by independent pipeline components. These results provide a practical screening criterion: autoencoder reconstruction quality, measurable without diffusion training, predicts downstream SR performance (R^2 = 0.67), suggesting that domain-specific VAE selection should precede diffusion architecture search. Code and trained model weights are publicly available at https://github.com/sebasmos/latent-sr.
affiliations: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
MIT Critical Data, Massachusetts Institute of Technology
Mbarara University of Science and Technology
Technical University of Applied Sciences Lubeck
University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

ru.title: Выбор правильного автокодировщика важнее архитектуры диффузии для медицинского сверхразрешения
ru.desc: В статье показано, что качество сверхразрешения медицинских изображений в моделях латентной диффузии в первую очередь зависит от выбора автокодировщика, а не от архитектуры самой диффузионной модели. Авторы заменили стандартный VAE из Stable Diffusion на MedVAE — специализированный автокодировщик, обученный на 1,6 миллионе медицинских изображений, что дало улучшение на 2,91-3,29 дБ по метрике PSNR. Проведённые эксперименты подтвердили, что качество восстановления изображения и уровень галлюцинаций контролируются независимыми компонентами конвейера обработки. Исследование предлагает практический критерий: качество восстановления автокодировщика можно измерить отдельно и оно предсказывает производительность итогового сверхразрешения с коэффициентом корреляции R² = 0,67.

en.title: Boosting Medical Image Quality with Domain-Specific Autoencoders
en.desc: This paper discusses how using specialized autoencoders, rather than generic variational autoencoders (VAEs), can enhance the quality of medical image super-resolution in latent diffusion models. The authors demonstrate that the choice of autoencoder significantly impacts reconstruction fidelity, which can be predicted based on the autoencoder's performance alone. By replacing the standard Stable Diffusion VAE with MedVAE, trained on a large dataset of medical images, they achieved notable improvements in image quality metrics. The findings suggest that selecting a domain-specific VAE should be prioritized before optimizing the diffusion model architecture for better results.

zh.title: 领域特定自编码器提升医学图像超分辨率
zh.desc: 本研究表明，在潜在扩散模型中，使用特定领域的自编码器（如MedVAE）替代通用变分自编码器（VAE），可以显著提高医学图像超分辨率的质量。通过对比实验，发现替换后的模型在膝关节MRI、脑MRI和胸部X光图像上，PSNR提升了2.91到3.29 dB。研究还表明，自编码器的重建质量可以预测下游超分辨率性能，且重建保真度与生成幻觉是由独立的管道组件控制的。这些结果提示，在选择扩散架构之前，应优先考虑领域特定的VAE。

papers.title: Beyond Perception Errors: Semantic Fixation in Large Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Vision-language models exhibit semantic fixation by preferring default interpretations over alternative valid rule mappings, which can be mitigated through prompt interventions and training strategies.  AI-generated summary Large vision-language models (VLMs) often rely on familiar semantic priors, but existing evaluations do not cleanly separate perception failures from rule-mapping failures. We study this behavior as semantic fixation: preserving a default interpretation even when the prompt specifies an alternative, equally valid mapping. To isolate this effect, we introduce VLM-Fix, a controlled benchmark over four abstract strategy games that evaluates identical terminal board states under paired standard and inverse rule formulations. Across 14 open and closed VLMs, accuracy consistently favors standard rules, revealing a robust semantic-fixation gap. Prompt interventions support this mechanism: neutral alias prompts substantially narrow the inverse-rule gap, while semantically loaded aliases reopen it. Post-training is strongly rule-aligned: training on one rule improves same-rule transfer but hurts opposite-rule transfer, while joint-rule training improves broader transfer. To test external validity beyond synthetic games, we evaluate analogous defamiliarization interventions on VLMBias and observe the same qualitative pattern. Finally, late-layer activation steering partially recovers degraded performance, indicating that semantic-fixation errors are at least partly editable in late representations. Project page, code, and dataset available at https://maveryn.github.io/vlm-fix/.

ru.title: Преодоление семантической фиксации в мультимодальных моделях через обучение и интервенции
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что большие мультимодальные модели зрения и языка страдают от семантической фиксации — склонности придерживаться привычных интерпретаций вместо альтернативных, но равно корректных правил. Авторы разработали контролируемый бенчмарк VLM-Fix, основанный на абстрактных стратегических играх, для изоляции этого эффекта от ошибок восприятия. Они демонстрируют, что простые интервенции с промптами — например использование нейтральных названий вместо семантически нагруженных — могут значительно снизить разрыв производительности при инверсных правилах. Кроме того, совместное обучение на обоих вариантах правил и управление активациями в поздних слоях сети позволяют улучшить обобщение модели и повысить её гибкость.

en.title: Breaking Semantic Fixation in Vision-Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates a phenomenon called semantic fixation in vision-language models (VLMs), where these models tend to stick to default interpretations instead of considering alternative valid mappings. The authors introduce a benchmark called VLM-Fix to evaluate this behavior in controlled settings using abstract strategy games. They find that VLMs show a consistent preference for standard rules over inverse rules, indicating a significant semantic-fixation gap. The study also explores interventions like prompt modifications and training strategies that can help mitigate this fixation, suggesting that model performance can be improved by adjusting late-layer activations.

zh.title: 打破语义固定，提升模型灵活性
zh.desc: 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLMs）中的语义固定现象，即模型在面对不同有效规则映射时，仍然偏好默认解释。我们提出了VLM-Fix基准，通过四个抽象策略游戏来评估模型在标准和逆规则下的表现差异。研究发现，模型在标准规则下的准确率明显高于逆规则，显示出语义固定的显著差距。通过提示干预和训练策略，可以减轻这种现象，表明语义固定错误在模型的后期表示中是可以部分修正的。

papers.title: Seeing Through Touch: Tactile-Driven Visual Localization of Material Regions
papers.abstract: Abstract Deep learning model for tactile localization that uses dense cross-modal feature interactions to identify material properties in images, overcoming limitations of existing methods through enhanced datasets and material-diversity pairing strategies.  AI-generated summary We address the problem of tactile localization, where the goal is to identify image regions that share the same material properties as a tactile input. Existing visuo-tactile methods rely on global alignment and thus fail to capture the fine-grained local correspondences required for this task. The challenge is amplified by existing datasets, which predominantly contain close-up, low-diversity images. We propose a model that learns local visuo-tactile alignment via dense cross-modal feature interactions, producing tactile saliency maps for touch-conditioned material segmentation. To overcome dataset constraints, we introduce: (i) in-the-wild multi-material scene images that expand visual diversity, and (ii) a material-diversity pairing strategy that aligns each tactile sample with visually varied yet tactilely consistent images, improving contextual localization and robustness to weak signals. We also construct two new tactile-grounded material segmentation datasets for quantitative evaluation. Experiments on both new and existing benchmarks show that our approach substantially outperforms prior visuo-tactile methods in tactile localization.
affiliations: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology
Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology

ru.title: Локальное выравнивание визуальных и тактильных признаков для сегментации материалов
ru.desc: В статье предлагается глубокая нейронная сеть для определения материалов по тактильным ощущениям, которая использует плотные кросс-модальные взаимодействия признаков для поиска областей изображений с одинаковыми материальными свойствами. Основное улучшение заключается в переходе от глобального выравнивания к локальному, позволяющему захватить тонкие соответствия между визуальными и тактильными данными. Авторы решают проблему ограниченного разнообразия существующих датасетов, предложив новые фотографии сцен с множеством материалов и стратегию подбора пар, где каждый тактильный образец соединяется с визуально разнообразными, но тактильно согласованными изображениями. Модель генерирует карты тактильной значимости для сегментации материалов и демонстрирует значительное превосходство над предыдущими методами на новых и существующих бенчмарках.

en.title: Enhancing Tactile Localization with Deep Learning and Diverse Datasets
en.desc: This paper presents a deep learning model designed for tactile localization, which aims to match image regions with specific material properties based on tactile input. The authors highlight the limitations of current visuo-tactile methods that rely on global alignment, which do not effectively capture the necessary local details. To address these challenges, they introduce dense cross-modal feature interactions and enhance the training datasets with diverse material images and a novel pairing strategy. Their experiments demonstrate significant improvements in tactile localization performance compared to existing methods, showcasing the effectiveness of their approach.

zh.title: 深度学习助力触觉定位的突破
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种深度学习模型，用于触觉定位，旨在通过密集的跨模态特征交互来识别图像中的材料属性。现有的视觉-触觉方法依赖于全局对齐，无法捕捉到任务所需的细粒度局部对应关系。为了解决数据集的限制，本文引入了多材料场景图像和材料多样性配对策略，以提高视觉多样性和上下文定位的准确性。实验结果表明，该方法在触觉定位任务上显著优于之前的视觉-触觉方法。

papers.title: 3DTV: A Feedforward Interpolation Network for Real-Time View Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract 3DTV combines lightweight geometry with learning for real-time sparse-view interpolation, achieving efficient and robust multi-view rendering without scene-specific optimization.  AI-generated summary Real-time free-viewpoint rendering requires balancing multi-camera redundancy with the latency constraints of interactive applications. We address this challenge by combining lightweight geometry with learning and propose 3DTV, a feedforward network for real-time sparse-view interpolation. A Delaunay-based triplet selection ensures angular coverage for each target view. Building on this, we introduce a pose-aware depth module that estimates a coarse-to-fine depth pyramid, enabling efficient feature reprojection and occlusion-aware blending. Unlike methods that require scene-specific optimization, 3DTV runs feedforward without retraining, making it practical for AR/VR, telepresence, and interactive applications. Our experiments on challenging multi-view video datasets demonstrate that 3DTV consistently achieves a strong balance of quality and efficiency, outperforming recent real-time novel-view baselines. Crucially, 3DTV avoids explicit proxies, enabling robust rendering across diverse scenes. This makes it a practical solution for low-latency multi-view streaming and interactive rendering.   Project Page: https://stefanmschulz.github.io/3DTV_webpage/

ru.title: Синтез произвольного видового ракурса в реальном времени без адаптации к сцене
ru.desc: 3DTV — это нейронная сеть для синтеза новых видов сцены в реальном времени на основе разреженного набора камер. Метод комбинирует легковесную геометрию с глубоким обучением, используя Делоне-триангуляцию для выбора опорных видов и модуль оценки глубины для точного переноса признаков. В отличие от других подходов, сеть работает без переобучения на конкретной сцене, что делает её практичной для AR/VR приложений и телеприсутствия. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что 3DTV достигает оптимального баланса между качеством синтеза и вычислительной эффективностью на различных видеодатасетах мультивью.

en.title: 3DTV: Real-Time Multi-View Rendering Made Easy
en.desc: The paper presents 3DTV, a novel feedforward network designed for real-time sparse-view interpolation in multi-view rendering. It effectively combines lightweight geometry with machine learning techniques to enhance rendering efficiency without the need for scene-specific optimization. The approach utilizes a Delaunay-based triplet selection for optimal angular coverage and incorporates a pose-aware depth module for accurate depth estimation. Experimental results show that 3DTV outperforms existing methods in terms of quality and efficiency, making it suitable for applications in AR/VR and interactive environments.

zh.title: 3DTV：高效实时多视图渲染的解决方案
zh.desc: 3DTV是一种结合轻量几何和学习的方法，用于实时稀疏视图插值，能够高效且稳健地进行多视图渲染，而无需场景特定的优化。该方法通过Delaunay三元组选择确保每个目标视图的角度覆盖，并引入了一个姿态感知深度模块，估计粗到细的深度金字塔，从而实现高效的特征重投影和遮挡感知混合。与需要场景特定优化的方法不同，3DTV采用前馈网络运行，无需重新训练，适用于增强现实、虚拟现实和交互式应用。实验结果表明，3DTV在质量和效率之间取得了良好的平衡，超越了最近的实时新视图基准。

papers.title: PokeRL: Reinforcement Learning for Pokemon Red
papers.abstract: Abstract PokeRL presents a modular reinforcement learning system with environment wrapping, anti-loop mechanisms, and hierarchical rewards to train agents for early-game Pokemon Red tasks.  AI-generated summary Pokemon Red is a long-horizon JRPG with sparse rewards, partial observability, and quirky control mechanics that make it a challenging benchmark for reinforcement learning. While recent work has shown that PPO agents can clear the first two gyms using heavy reward shaping and engineered observations, training remains brittle in practice, with agents often degenerating into action loops, menu spam, or unproductive wandering. In this paper, we present PokeRL, a modular system that trains deep reinforcement learning agents to complete early game tasks in Pokemon Red, including exiting the player's house, exploring Pallet Town to reach tall grass, and winning the first rival battle. Our main contributions are a loop-aware environment wrapper around the PyBoy emulator with map masking, a multi-layer anti-loop and anti-spam mechanism, and a dense hierarchical reward design. We argue that practical systems like PokeRL, which explicitly model failure modes such as loops and spam, are a necessary intermediate step between toy benchmarks and full Pokemon League champion agents. Code is available at https://github.com/reddheeraj/PokemonRL
affiliations: Department of Computer Science, Texas A&M University
Texas A&M Institute of Data Science, Texas A&M University

ru.title: Укрощение цикличности: модульный RL-агент для Pokemon Red
ru.desc: PokeRL — это модульная система глубокого обучения с подкреплением для тренировки агентов на ранних этапах игры Pokemon Red. Система решает критические проблемы обучения, такие как зацикливание агента, спам меню и непродуктивное блуждание, используя специализированный враппер окружения с механизмом обнаружения петель. Авторы предлагают многоуровневый подход с анти-спам механизмом и иерархической функцией вознаграждения, которая способствует более стабильному обучению. PokeRL демонстрирует, что явное моделирование типичных режимов отказа необходимо для создания практических систем обучения с подкреплением на сложных долгосрочных задачах.

en.title: Mastering Pokemon Red with Smart Reinforcement Learning!
en.desc: PokeRL is a modular reinforcement learning system designed to train agents for early-game tasks in Pokemon Red, a challenging game with sparse rewards and complex mechanics. The system includes an environment wrapper that helps prevent agents from getting stuck in repetitive actions, known as loops, and incorporates a hierarchical reward structure to guide learning. By addressing common pitfalls in training, such as menu spam and unproductive wandering, PokeRL aims to improve the robustness of agents in long-horizon tasks. This work highlights the importance of modeling failure modes in reinforcement learning to bridge the gap between simple benchmarks and more complex gaming scenarios.

zh.title: PokeRL：强化学习的新突破
zh.desc: PokeRL是一个模块化的强化学习系统，旨在训练智能体完成《精灵宝可梦红版》早期游戏任务。该系统通过环境包装、反循环机制和分层奖励设计，解决了稀疏奖励和部分可观察性的问题。PokeRL的主要创新在于其能够有效防止智能体陷入无效的动作循环和菜单刷屏。我们认为，像PokeRL这样的实用系统是从简单基准到完整的宝可梦联盟冠军智能体之间的重要一步。

papers.title: When Reasoning Models Hurt Behavioral Simulation: A Solver-Sampler Mismatch in Multi-Agent LLM Negotiation
papers.abstract: Abstract Reasoning-enhanced large language models can perform poorly as simulators of boundedly rational behavior, exhibiting over-optimization and reduced diversity compared to models using bounded reflection strategies.  AI-generated summary Large language models are increasingly used as agents in social, economic, and policy simulations. A common assumption is that stronger reasoning should improve simulation fidelity. We argue that this assumption can fail when the objective is not to solve a strategic problem, but to sample plausible boundedly rational behavior. In such settings, reasoning-enhanced models can become better solvers and worse simulators: they can over-optimize for strategically dominant actions, collapse compromise-oriented terminal behavior, and sometimes exhibit a diversity-without-fidelity pattern in which local variation survives without outcome-level fidelity. We study this solver-sampler mismatch in three multi-agent negotiation environments adapted from earlier simulation work: an ambiguous fragmented-authority trading-limits scenario, an ambiguous unified-opposition trading-limits scenario, and a new-domain grid-curtailment case in emergency electricity management. We compare three reflection conditions, no reflection, bounded reflection, and native reasoning, across two primary model families and then extend the same protocol to direct OpenAI runs with GPT-4.1 and GPT-5.2. Across all three experiments, bounded reflection produces substantially more diverse and compromise-oriented trajectories than either no reflection or native reasoning. In the direct OpenAI extension, GPT-5.2 native ends in authority decisions in 45 of 45 runs across the three experiments, while GPT-5.2 bounded recovers compromise outcomes in every environment. The contribution is not a claim that reasoning is generally harmful. It is a methodological warning: model capability and simulation fidelity are different objectives, and behavioral simulation should qualify models as samplers, not only as solvers.

ru.title: Рассуждение вредит симуляции: когда способность моделей становится их слабостью
ru.desc: В статье исследуется парадокс, когда языковые модели с усиленными способностями к рассуждению становятся хуже симуляторами ограниченно рациональной человеческой поведения в многоагентных переговорных сценариях. Авторы показывают, что модели с полноценным рассуждением склонны к чрезмерной оптимизации и выбору стратегически доминирующих действий, что приводит к снижению разнообразия и реалистичности результатов. Решение заключается в использовании ограниченной рефлексии, которая позволяет генерировать более разнообразные и компромиссные траектории поведения. Исследование проводилось на трёх сценариях многоагентного взаимодействия и показало, что ограниченная рефлексия значительно превосходит как отсутствие рефлексии, так и полноценное рассуждение в задачах поведенческой симуляции.

en.title: Balancing Reasoning and Diversity in AI Simulations
en.desc: This paper discusses how large language models (LLMs) can struggle when used to simulate realistic human behavior in decision-making scenarios. It highlights that while enhanced reasoning abilities in these models may improve their problem-solving skills, they can lead to over-optimization and a lack of diversity in their simulated actions. The authors demonstrate that using bounded reflection strategies results in more varied and compromise-oriented outcomes compared to models relying solely on native reasoning. Ultimately, the paper warns that the goals of improving model capabilities and achieving accurate behavioral simulations are not the same, suggesting that models should be evaluated on their ability to sample diverse behaviors rather than just solve problems.

zh.title: 推理与模拟：模型能力与模拟准确性并不相同
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了增强推理的大型语言模型在模拟有限理性行为时的表现。研究发现，虽然更强的推理能力通常被认为能提高模拟的准确性，但在某些情况下，这种假设可能不成立。特别是在需要采样合理的有限理性行为时，增强推理的模型可能会过度优化，导致缺乏多样性。作者通过多个多智能体谈判环境的实验，表明有限反思策略能产生更丰富和妥协导向的结果。

papers.title: Spatial Competence Benchmark
papers.abstract: Abstract Three frontier models show declining accuracy on a new spatial competence benchmark, with performance saturating quickly under token budget constraints.  AI-generated summary Spatial competence is the quality of maintaining a consistent internal representation of an environment and using it to infer discrete structure and plan actions under constraints. Prevailing spatial evaluations for large models are limited to probing isolated primitives through 3D transformations or visual question answering. We introduce the Spatial Competence Benchmark (SCBench), spanning three hierarchical capability buckets whose tasks require executable outputs verified by deterministic checkers or simulator-based evaluators. On SCBench, three frontier models exhibit monotonically decreasing accuracy up the capability ladder. Sweeping output-token caps shows that accuracy gains concentrate at low budgets and saturate quickly, and failures are dominated by locally plausible geometry that breaks global constraints. We release the task generators, verifiers, and visualisation tooling.

ru.title: Пространственное мышление LLM: быстрое насыщение способностей при ограничении контекста
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый бенчмарк SCBench для оценки пространственной компетентности больших языковых моделей, который включает задачи с иерархической сложностью, требующие проверяемых выходных результатов. Исследование показало, что три передовых модели демонстрируют монотонное снижение точности по мере увеличения сложности задач. Анализ влияния ограничения на количество токенов выявил, что прирост точности концентрируется при низких бюджетах и быстро насыщается, а ошибки в основном связаны с локально правдоподобной геометрией, нарушающей глобальные ограничения. Авторы делают доступными генераторы задач, верификаторы и инструменты визуализации для сообщества.

en.title: Evaluating Spatial Competence: Models Struggle with Complexity
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of current large machine learning models in spatial competence, which is their ability to understand and interact with environments. The authors introduce a new evaluation tool called the Spatial Competence Benchmark (SCBench) that tests models on various tasks requiring accurate spatial reasoning. They found that three leading models showed decreasing accuracy as task complexity increased, particularly when constrained by output token limits. The results indicate that while models perform well on simpler tasks, they struggle with maintaining global spatial consistency in more complex scenarios.

zh.title: 空间能力基准：评估模型的空间理解
zh.desc: 本文介绍了空间能力基准（SCBench），用于评估大型模型在空间理解方面的表现。研究发现，三种前沿模型在新的基准测试中准确率逐渐下降，尤其是在输出令牌预算有限的情况下。模型在低预算时的准确率提升明显，但很快就达到饱和，且错误主要源于局部几何形状的合理性破坏了全局约束。我们还发布了任务生成器、验证器和可视化工具，以便于进一步研究。

papers.title: SpotSound: Enhancing Large Audio-Language Models with Fine-Grained Temporal Grounding
papers.abstract: Abstract SpotSound is an audio language model that improves temporal grounding in long-form audio by suppressing false timestamps and addressing challenges posed by sparse events in noisy backgrounds.  AI-generated summary Large Audio-Language Models (ALMs) have recently demonstrated remarkable capabilities in holistic audio understanding, yet they remain unreliable for temporal grounding, i.e., the task of pinpointing exactly when an event occurs within long-form audio. This limitation stems from two factors: training data dominated by clip-level supervision lacking precise timestamps, and benchmarks that fail to simulate real-world scenarios where short events are obscured by dense background sounds. In this paper, we introduce SpotSound, an audio language model designed for grounding audio events. SpotSound incorporates a novel training objective, specifically designed to suppress hallucinated timestamps for events absent from the input. Additionally, we present SpotSound-Bench, a challenging temporal grounding benchmark where target events occupy less than ~10\% of each clip, creating a rigorous `needle-in-a-haystack' evaluation. Experiments demonstrate that SpotSound achieves state-of-the-art results on temporal grounding benchmarks while maintaining robust performance across general downstream audio-language tasks. Code, models and benchmark are released on https://loiesun.github.io/spotsound/

ru.title: Точная локализация редких событий в аудио через подавление галлюцинаций
ru.desc: SpotSound — это аудио-языковая модель, которая улучшает временную локализацию событий в длинных аудиозаписях. Модель решает проблему галлюцинаций временных меток для отсутствующих событий и справляется с редкими событиями на фоне шума. Авторы предложили новую функцию потерь, которая подавляет ложные временные метки, и создали сложный бенчмарк SpotSound-Bench для оценки. Экспериментально показано, что SpotSound достигает лучших результатов в задачах временной локализации при сохранении производительности на других задачах понимания аудио.

en.title: SpotSound: Precision in Audio Event Timing
en.desc: SpotSound is an innovative audio language model that enhances the ability to accurately identify when events occur in long audio recordings. It addresses the common issue of false timestamps and the difficulty of detecting sparse events amidst noisy backgrounds. The model uses a unique training approach that minimizes incorrect timestamp predictions for events that are not present in the audio. Additionally, SpotSound-Bench provides a rigorous testing framework that challenges the model with scenarios where target events are rare, demonstrating SpotSound's superior performance in temporal grounding tasks.

zh.title: SpotSound：提升音频事件时间定位的创新模型
zh.desc: SpotSound是一种音频语言模型，旨在改善长音频中的时间定位能力。它通过抑制虚假时间戳和解决嘈杂背景中稀疏事件带来的挑战来实现这一目标。该模型引入了一种新的训练目标，专门设计用于抑制输入中缺失事件的虚假时间戳。此外，SpotSound-Bench是一个具有挑战性的时间定位基准，目标事件在每个片段中占比不到10%。

papers.title: CONSCIENTIA: Can LLM Agents Learn to Strategize? Emergent Deception and Trust in a Multi-Agent NYC Simulation
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language model agents demonstrate limited strategic behaviors including selective trust and deception in a simulated urban environment, remaining vulnerable to adversarial persuasion despite improved resistance over iterations.  AI-generated summary As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents, understanding how strategic behavior emerges in multi-agent environments has become an important alignment challenge. We take a neutral empirical stance and construct a controlled environment in which strategic behavior can be directly observed and measured. We introduce a large-scale multi-agent simulation in a simplified model of New York City, where LLM-driven agents interact under opposing incentives. Blue agents aim to reach their destinations efficiently, while Red agents attempt to divert them toward billboard-heavy routes using persuasive language to maximize advertising revenue. Hidden identities make navigation socially mediated, forcing agents to decide when to trust or deceive. We study policy learning through an iterative simulation pipeline that updates agent policies across repeated interaction rounds using Kahneman-Tversky Optimization (KTO). Blue agents are optimized to reduce billboard exposure while preserving navigation efficiency, whereas Red agents adapt to exploit remaining weaknesses. Across iterations, the best Blue policy improves task success from 46.0% to 57.3%, although susceptibility remains high at 70.7%. Later policies exhibit stronger selective cooperation while preserving trajectory efficiency. However, a persistent safety-helpfulness trade-off remains: policies that better resist adversarial steering do not simultaneously maximize task completion. Overall, our results show that LLM agents can exhibit limited strategic behavior, including selective trust and deception, while remaining highly vulnerable to adversarial persuasion.
affiliations: AI Institute, University of South Carolina

ru.title: Стратегическое поведение LLM-агентов в многоагентной среде остаётся уязвимым к манипуляциям
ru.desc: Исследователи создали крупномасштабную мультиагентную симуляцию, где LLM-агенты взаимодействуют с противоположными целями: одни стремятся эффективно достичь пункта назначения, другие пытаются убедить их идти по маршрутам с рекламой. Агенты обучались с помощью метода оптимизации Kahneman-Tversky Optimization через итеративный процесс обновления политик поведения. Результаты показали, что LLM-агенты демонстрируют ограниченные стратегические поведения, включая избирательное доверие и обман, но остаются уязвимы к противниковому воздействию. Работа выявила фундаментальный конфликт между устойчивостью к манипуляциям и эффективностью выполнения основной задачи.

en.title: Navigating Trust and Deception in LLM Agents
en.desc: This paper explores how large language model (LLM) agents behave strategically in a simulated urban environment. The study focuses on the interactions between two types of agents: Blue agents, who aim to navigate efficiently, and Red agents, who use persuasive tactics to divert them for advertising purposes. Through iterative policy learning, the Blue agents improve their success rate in avoiding distractions, but they still show significant vulnerability to manipulation. The findings highlight the challenges of aligning LLM agents' behaviors, as they can exhibit selective trust and deception while struggling with a trade-off between safety and task completion.

zh.title: 大型语言模型的战略行为与脆弱性
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了大型语言模型（LLM）在多智能体环境中的战略行为。我们构建了一个简化的纽约市模型，蓝色代理旨在高效到达目的地，而红色代理则通过说服性语言试图引导蓝色代理走向广告密集的路线。通过迭代模拟，我们观察到蓝色代理在减少广告暴露的同时提高了任务成功率，但仍然对对手的说服保持高度脆弱。研究结果表明，LLM代理能够表现出有限的战略行为，包括选择性信任和欺骗。
